title: Extension app glossary
text: This page defines common terms used in reference to extension apps.

title: Extension app glossary
text:
Actions are the individual functions that do the work in your extension app. They specify the external platform endpoints to call from your Docusign envelopes and workflows. An action’s definition must include:

- A specific external platform endpoint to call when the action is triggered
- The required parameters to make the call

If registering an extension app via a manifest file:

- A reference to the external platform connection to use for the action
- A template identifier that specifies the action contract. This determines the request and response contract to be used as well as the extension points that will trigger the action. Template identifiers are predefined by Docusign.

Actions are executed at a defined point (an extension point) in a Docusign envelope or workflow when specified by an extension app.

title: Extension app glossary
text: An intermediary server that translates between external APIs and Docusign. An API proxy is needed only if requests to and responses from the external service endpoints do not conform to the Docusign action or capability contract schema. The proxy provides a mapping that transforms extension app requests to the external API's required format, and transforms the API’s responses to the format that Docusign expects.

title: Extension app glossary
text: Before an extension app can be published in the Docusign App Center, it must pass a Docusign review. The review process verifies that the app complies with Docusign policies for security and API usage. Once an extension app passes Docusign review, it is approved and the developer can publish it to the App Center.

title: Extension app glossary
text: A tool, accessible through the Docusign user experience, that enables Docusign users to browse, find, and install Docusign extension apps. It is the place to which developers publish their extension apps for public consumption or for use by selected customers. See [Choosing private distribution instead of public](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/choosing-private-distribution/) for more information.

title: Extension app glossary
text: A tool in the Developer Console with which developers can register and edit their extension app manifest, a JSON file that specifies the elements of an extension app. The app manifest editor provides features to edit your app manifest, download or copy it as a file, format the contents, and validate the JSON.

title: Extension app glossary
text: Capabilities provide optional features that can be added to an extension's base functionality. While [actions](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/concepts/actions/) are required, capabilities are optional. Capabilities can be implemented alongside the required actions to incorporate additional functionality into your extension app.

title: Extension app glossary
text: An object, configured in the form-based extension app registration experience or specified in an extension app’s manifest file, that holds the parameters required to connect to an external platform’s APIs. Typically, this will contain authorization data for the account that will make the API calls on that platform.

title: Extension app glossary
text:
The developer tool for managing the entire lifecycle of extension apps. The Developer Console provides features to accomplish the following with extension apps:

- Register: Specify the app’s connection, extensions, actions, and capabilities
- Test: Verify the ability to connect to external APIs and exchange information correctly, and execute in the context of a Docusign envelope or workflow
- Manage: Review, modify, update, or delete
- Submit for Docusign review prior to publishing
- Publish to the App Center for public use or distribution to selected customers

title: Extension app glossary
text: A group of actions and capabilities that support a type of external functionality, such as data verification or file output cloud storage. Each extension has an *extension contract* (set by the extension object’s template, specified in the manifest file by its template property) that identifies its type and the required actions or capabilities that you must implement to use it.

title: Extension app glossary
text: A type of partner application that enables a developer to enhance the functionality of Docusign envelopes or workflows by incorporating features from external platforms, such as Google Drive or Stripe, through calling their APIs. In contrast to API integrations, which are self-contained and distributed, hosted, or installed by their own developers, extension apps execute on the Docusign platform, are accessed through the Docusign App Center, and function within the Docusign user experience.

title: Extension app glossary
text: A JSON file that provides metadata used to specify the elements of an extension app: its connection, its extensions, and its actions and capabilities, as well as metadata such as a title, description, icon, and screenshots that provide data needed for its being listed in the App Center. Extension app manifest files are validated against the JSON extension app manifest schema, maintained by Docusign, before they are approved for publication.

title: Extension app glossary
text: A place in a Docusign core functionality (such as an eSignature envelope or Agreement Builder workflow), predefined by Docusign, at which an action or capability can be invoked. When execution reaches an extension point, any relevant actions belonging to enabled extensions will be triggered.

title: Extension app glossary
text: In the Developer Console, a user experience that enables extension app developers to register an extension app by configuring the app’s properties in a [form](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/register/use-form/). The form enables the developer to specify the app’s connection, extensions, actions, and capabilities; the properties thus specified are then used by the Developer Console to register an extension app manifest.

title: Extension app glossary
text: Agreement Builder is a Docusign platform service that enables customers to build and digitize their agreement processes, called *workflows*, with Docusign and partner services. Docusign provides the Agreement Builder UI, an interactive workflow builder, to enable users to create and manage such workflows without using code; and the Agreement Builder API, which gives developers the ability to do the same from within their Docusign integrations.

title: Extension app glossary
text: The Agreement Builder API is an interface that enables developers to manage their automated workflows programmatically through REST-based calls.

title: Extension app glossary
text: A foundational component of Docusign Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM), Docusign Agreement Manager is a platform service in the form of a smart repository built specifically to tackle the complexities of agreement management. More than just document storage, Agreement Manager is powered by Docusign AI, which identifies and stores key attributes of an agreement and makes them available for searching, filtering and serving up powerful insights.

title: Extension app glossary
text: The Agreement Manager API is an interface that enables developers to query Agreement Manager for AI-extracted data from the Agreement Manager smart agreement repository.

title: Extension app glossary
text: Private apps are extension apps published to the App Center in private mode. Developers can make these private apps available to specific accounts, allowing those accounts to adopt the extension and integrate it into their own Agreement Builder workflows. Private apps are only available to the accounts specified by the developer; accounts that haven’t been given access to the app cannot view or install it from the App Center. Before being published in private mode, extension apps must first pass a Docusign review.

title: Extension app glossary
text: Public apps are extension apps that have been [published to the App Center](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/publish/) in public mode, and therefore available for use by all Docusign customers. Publishing to the App Center makes an extension app discoverable by other Docusign users in other accounts, who can then adopt the extension app and incorporate it into their own Agreement Builder workflows. To be published, extension apps must first pass Docusign review.

title: Extension app glossary
text: An example implementation for a specific use case, developed by Docusign to help extension app developers learn how to work with extensions and build their own extension apps. Reference implementations are published as code repositories (“repos”) on the [Docusign GitHub](https://github.com/docusign) project.

title: Extension app glossary
text: The default app manifest is loaded in the manifest editor as a developer attempts to register a new extension app. The default app manifest has sample data that can be used for testing, and it can be updated to reflect the real values of the application you wish to build.

title: Extension app glossary
text: Docusign offers developers the means to test their extension apps before publishing. When viewing an extension app in the Developer Console, a developer can select App Testing and then choose from automated test options offered by Docusign that include testing the app’s connection properties as well as its ability to call all the API endpoints called by the extension. From the Developer Console, a developer can also launch the App Center to test the display of the app’s information, install it to their account, and ensure that it behaves correctly when invoked from within Docusign.

title: Extension app glossary
text: A sequence of execution steps that you can define and call in your apps. This sequence of steps can include Docusign functionality such as getting user information using web forms, requesting a signature, conditional logic, and triggering extensions from enabled extension apps. Workflows are created and managed through the Agreement Builder API and the [Workflow Designer](https://apps-d.docusign.com/send/workflows/).

title: Extension app glossary
text:
When a workflow is triggered, an individual copy of its definition is activated. A workflow instance is created for a specific set of participants and holds the data for their agreement process.

title: Support resources and FAQs
text: Think of extension apps as modules, or plugins, that integrate seamlessly with the Docusign Agreement Platform.  An extension app incorporates external features into your eSignature agreements or Docusign workflows by making requests to the external API. Once you've built an extension app, you can make it available to the entire Docusign global customer base in our App Center. See [Extension apps 101 overview](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/) for more information.

title: Support resources and FAQs
heading_path: How are extension apps different from existing Docusign API integrations?
text: By integrating with Docusign APIs, you can incorporate Docusign functionality into your app. By building extension apps, you can create custom capabilities and bring them into the Docusign agreement process via specific extension points. Some examples of extension points include eSignature agreements and steps in Docusign workflows, such as an envelope being sent or a document being signed.

title: Support resources and FAQs
heading_path: What user permissions are needed to create extension apps?
text: Anyone with admin permissions in a developer account can create extension apps.

title: Support resources and FAQs
text: Admins from any organization can install apps. To install on the demo environment, a developer account is required. To install on the production environment, a production account is required. [Private apps](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/choosing-private-distribution/) can only be viewed, installed, and used by accounts specified by the app’s developer.

title: Support resources and FAQs
heading_path: How can extension apps be used in Docusign workflows?
text: Extension app functionality can be invoked from eSignature agreements and from Workflow Builder. The [Workflow Builder API](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/workflow-builder-api/) empowers customers to build and deploy streamlined agreement workflows, seamlessly integrating Docusign capabilities and external services. Customers can install an extension app to their Docusign account, and then configure workflows to trigger the extension app at the appropriate step in the workflow. To learn more, read about extension apps in the [developer documentation](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/).

title: Support resources and FAQs
heading_path: Why should I develop extension apps for Docusign agreement workflows?
text: Building extension apps enables you to provide tailored solutions, automate processes, and streamline agreement workflows for Docusign users. By distributing your apps on the App Center, you can expand your reach over 1.4 million Docusign customers.

title: Support resources and FAQs
heading_path: What technologies do I need to know to create extension apps?
text: At a high level, building extension apps requires developers to have a sound understanding of HTTP protocols, server-side programming languages (for example, Python or Node.js), cloud hosting platforms, OAuth authorization protocols, and more. They will need to set up a publicly accessible endpoint (URL) to receive incoming extension payloads, parse and process JSON data, and execute specific actions or workflows. For more information please read the [extension app documentation](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/).

title: Support resources and FAQs
heading_path: Does Docusign provide developer tools for building extension apps?
text: Yes. The Docusign Developer Console enables developers to create, test, and manage extension apps, as well as submit them for a review process that's a prerequisite for publishing them to the Docusign App Center. See [Developer Console overview](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/developer-console-overview/)  for more information.

title: Support resources and FAQs
heading_path: How do I test my extension app before deployment?
text: Developers can use the Dev Console testing feature to simulate and validate the behavior of their extension apps within Docusign agreement workflows.

title: Support resources and FAQs
heading_path: Can I take a course to learn how to build and publish extension apps?
text: Yes, Docusign University offers a self-paced course: [Build Extension Apps for the Docusign App Center](https://dsudevelopers.docusign.com/build-extension-apps-for-the-docusign-app-center).

title: Support resources and FAQs
heading_path: I have a technical question about building extension apps. How can I contact technical support?
text: Please visit the [Docusign Developer Community](https://community.docusign.com/developer-59) for information and assistance.

title: Support resources and FAQs
heading_path: What are my options for publishing extension apps?
text: You can choose to publish your app in public mode, which makes it available to all Docusign customers, or in [private](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/choosing-private-distribution/) mode, which makes it available only to Docusign accounts you specify.

title: Support resources and FAQs
heading_path: What is the process for publishing extension apps to the Docusign App Center?
text: Once your extension app is ready, you can [submit it for review](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/publish/). After it has passed the Docusign review process, you'll be able to list it on the Docusign App Center.

title: Support resources and FAQs
heading_path: Can I monetize my extension apps on the Docusign App Center?
text: Currently, the Docusign App Center does not support direct monetization of extension apps.

title: Support resources and FAQs
heading_path: How can I stay current with extension app development best practices and updates from Docusign?
text:
Subscribe to our [Newsletter](https://developers.docusign.com/newsletter/) and follow our [Blog](https://www.docusign.com/blog/developers) to receive the latest news, tips, and insights on extension app development with Docusign.

title: Troubleshooting
text:
This topic provides troubleshooting guidance for these types of errors:

- [Connection test errors](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/troubleshooting/#connection-test-errors)
- [Extension test errors](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/troubleshooting/#extension-test-errors)
- [Errors caused by an ad blocker](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/troubleshooting/#errors-caused-by-an-ad-blocker)

title: Troubleshooting
text: The errors below may be displayed when you [run a connection test](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/test/integration-tests/#run-a-connection-test) on the Developer Console Testing page.

title: Troubleshooting
text:
e displayed when you [run a connection test](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/test/integration-tests/#run-a-connection-test) on the Developer Console Testing page.

| **Error message** | **Description** | **Troubleshooting** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| A refresh token was not returned from the token server. | The API service or the identity provider has not been configured to supply refresh tokens. | Check the API service documentation to confirm that refresh tokens are supported and to find out what is required to enable them. If you’re using an identity provider, make sure that it's configured to allow refresh tokens. See [Authorization](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/it-infrastructure/authorization/) for more information. |
| There was an error creating the connection instance. | An internal system error occurred when the API service or the identity provider returned an authorization code to the Docusign platform. | Retry the test. If the issue is persistent, please visit the [Docusign Developer Community](https://community.docusign.com/developer-59) for information and assistance. |
| There was an error creating the OAuth token. | An internal system error occurred when the API service or the identity provider returned an authorization code to the Docusign platform. | Retry the test. If the issue is persistent, please visit the [Docusign Developer Community](https://community.docusign.com/developer-59) for information and assistance. |
| There was an error exchanging the authorization code for a token. | The Docusign platform was unable to exchange the authorization code for an access token. This can be caused by:  - An invalid token URL in the extension app manifest - An invalid or expired authorization code returned by the API service or the identity provider - A network or internet issue when the Docusign platform attempted to exchange the authorization code for an access token - The authorization service or identity provider service not being available | - Check the [Token URL](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/register/use-form/integration-details/#update-connection-settings) in the connection settings or the [connections.params.customConfig.tokenUrl](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-app-reference/app-manifest-reference/connection/#connection-custom-configuration-schema) in the extension app manifest and make sure that the value is correct. - Check the authorized user's account with the API service or identity provider, and make sure that the user has permissions for all of the [Scopes](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/register/use-form/integration-details/#update-connection-settings) specified in the connection settings or the [scopes](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-app-reference/app-manifest-reference/connection/#connection-parameters-schema) specified in the extension app manifest. - Confirm that the authorization service or identity provider service is running. - Confirm that the API service or identity provider firewall is configured to allow requests from Docusign IP addresses. See [Firewall configuration](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/it-infrastructure/network-configuration/#firewall-configuration) for details. |
| There was an error persisting the OAuth token. | While exchanging the authorization code for an access token, the Docusign platform was unable to save the access token, possibly due to network issues. | Retry the test. If the issue is persistent, please visit the [Docusign Developer Community](https://community.docusign.com/developer-59) for information and assistance. |
| There was a problem initializing the connection test. | The Docusign platform was unable to generate the authorization URL or initiate the connection test. | Retry the test. If the issue is persistent, please visit the [Docusign Developer Community](https://community.docusign.com/developer-59) for information and assistance. |

title: Troubleshooting
text:
The errors below may be displayed when you [run an extension test](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/test/integration-tests/#run-an-extension-test) on the Developer Console Testing page.

| **Error message** | **Description** | **Troubleshooting** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [error from API service] | Docusign successfully sent the request, but an error response was returned. The Developer Console displays the error details received from the API service. | - Review the API service documentation to determine why it could not process the request. - For HTTP errors such as 403 Forbidden, 404 Not Found, 502 Bad Gateway, or 503 Service Unavailable, confirm that the API service firewall is configured to allow requests from Docusign IP addresses. See [Firewall configuration](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/it-infrastructure/network-configuration/#firewall-configuration) for details. |
| Error executing the extension | The JSON request body is missing a required property, includes a property that is invalid, or otherwise does not conform to the expected schema. | Check the [action](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/concepts/actions/) or [capability](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/concepts/capabilities/) contract for the request, update the request body if it doesn't conform to the contract, and retry the test.  You can access the action and capability contracts for each extension from the [Extension contracts](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-app-reference/extension-contracts/) page. |
| Error scheduling execution | An internal system error occurred. | Retry the test. If the issue is persistent, please visit the [Docusign Developer Community](https://community.docusign.com/developer-59) for information and assistance. |
| Failed to insert actionTest: createActionTest - AppVersion with id [ID] could not be found. | The extension app manifest has been updated since the extension app was installed to your account. | Uninstall the extension app and reinstall it. See [Run a connection test](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/test/integration-tests/#run-a-connection-test) for installation instructions. |
| Failed to insert actionTest: Invalid record returned | An internal system error occurred. | Retry the test. If the issue is persistent, please visit the [Docusign Developer Community](https://community.docusign.com/developer-59) for information and assistance. |
| User Error: Request failed with status code 404 | The Docusign platform was unable to reach the endpoint path. | - Check [Params URI](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/register/use-form/integration-details/#update-extension-and-action-settings) in the [action](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/concepts/actions/) or [capability](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/concepts/capabilities/) settings or [actions.params.uri](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-app-reference/app-manifest-reference/action/) in the extension app manifest and make sure that it's correct and reachable. - Confirm that the API service firewall is configured to allow requests from Docusign IP addresses. See [Firewall configuration](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/it-infrastructure/network-configuration/#firewall-configuration) for details. |

title: Troubleshooting
heading_path: Errors caused by an ad blocker
text:
When you try to install an extension app to your account in the [App Center](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/app-center/) or uninstall an app, this error message is displayed:

`We encountered an unexpected error. Reload the page and try again.`

title: Troubleshooting
heading_path: Errors caused by an ad blocker
text: This issue can occur when your device or network is running an ad blocker.

title: Troubleshooting
heading_path: Errors caused by an ad blocker
text:
Either disable the ad blocker, or add [these domains](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/it-infrastructure/network-configuration/#ad-blocker-configuration) to the allowed list for the ad blocker.

If disabling the ad blocker or adding the domains to the allowed list doesn't resolve the issue, please visit the [Docusign Developer Community](https://community.docusign.com/developer-59) for information and assistance.

title: Troubleshooting for connected fields extensions
text: This topic provides troubleshooting guidance for issues that may arise when working with [connected fields extensions](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/supported-extensions/connected-fields/) in eSignature or in the [Docusign App Center](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/app-center/).

title: Troubleshooting for connected fields extensions
heading_path: eSignature envelope or template preparation issues
text: In the eSignature [Add Fields view](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=odn1578456409392.html), **App Fields** does not appear after you select **Custom Fields**, or you do not see your extension app name under **App Fields**. Connected fields extension apps should appear under **App Fields**.

title: Troubleshooting for connected fields extensions
heading_path: eSignature envelope or template preparation issues
text:
- The extension app may not be installed or connected to your account. See [Run a connection test](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/test/integration-tests/#run-a-connection-test) for instructions to install and connect it in the Developer Console, or [install and connect](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html) it to your account in the App Center.
- When the extension app was connected to your account, Docusign was unable to process the [Get Type Names](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-app-reference/extension-contracts/connected-fields/#get-type-names) or [Get Type Definitions](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-app-reference/extension-contracts/connected-fields/#get-type-definitions) response, possibly due to an error in the data model. To check this, run the [extension tests](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/test/integration-tests/#run-an-extension-test) for these two actions. You can use the [Concerto VSCode extension](https://concerto.accordproject.org/docs/tools/vscode/) to validate your data model and check for errors.
- The connection to the external service was invalidated due to authorization issues or deleted. [Re-establish](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=zsb1714159452358.html) the connection for the extension app in the Docusign App Center. See [Connection behaviors and best practices](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/best-practices/connection-behaviors-and-best-practices/) for more information.
- You are working with an eSignature template that you added to your account by uploading a template that was downloaded from another account. However, the account to which you uploaded the template does not have the connected fields extension app installed or connected. See [Run a connection test](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/test/integration-tests/#run-a-connection-test) for instructions to install and connect it in the Developer Console, or [install and connect](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html) it to your account in the App Center.

title: Troubleshooting for connected fields extensions
heading_path: eSignature envelope or template preparation issues
text: In the eSignature [Add Fields view](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=odn1578456409392.html), the list of available connected fields for your extension app is out of date.

title: Troubleshooting for connected fields extensions
heading_path: eSignature envelope or template preparation issues
text:
- The data model changed after the extension app was connected to your account. [Refresh the connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=qkv1716320270335.html) for the extension app in the Docusign App Center. This keeps the connection intact and causes Docusign to execute [Get Type Names](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-app-reference/extension-contracts/connected-fields/#get-type-names) and [Get Type Definitions](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-app-reference/extension-contracts/connected-fields/#get-type-definitions) actions to retrieve the latest data model.
- When the extension app was connected to your account, Docusign was unable to process the **Get Type Names** or **Get Type Definitions** response, possibly due to an error in the data model. To check this, run the [extension tests](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/test/integration-tests/#run-an-extension-test) for these two actions. You can use the [Concerto VSCode extension](https://concerto.accordproject.org/docs/tools/vscode/) to validate your data model and check for errors.

title: Troubleshooting for connected fields extensions
heading_path: eSignature envelope or template preparation issues
text: In the eSignature [Add Fields view](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=odn1578456409392.html), multiple copies of the extension app are listed with different connection names, but you only expect to see one listing.

title: Troubleshooting for connected fields extensions
heading_path: eSignature envelope or template preparation issues
text: Repeatedly running [connection tests](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/test/integration-tests/#run-a-connection-test) on the Developer Console Testing page causes multiple connections to be created. You can [delete connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=pnb1716320193292.html) in the App Center. For an extension app in **Draft** status, you can [uninstall and reinstall](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/test/integration-tests/#run-a-connection-test) it on the Developer Console Testing page, and then run a new connection test to create a single connection. Because uninstalling and reinstalling in the Developer Console deletes all existing connections, this option is not recommended for extension apps that are being used in production. See [Connection behaviors and best practices](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/best-practices/connection-behaviors-and-best-practices/) for more information.

title: Troubleshooting for connected fields extensions
heading_path: eSignature envelope or template preparation issues
text: In the eSignature [Add Fields view](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=odn1578456409392.html), a connected field is displayed as required (indicated by an asterisk), but it should be optional.

title: Troubleshooting for connected fields extensions
heading_path: eSignature envelope or template preparation issues
text:
- The property is marked with the [IsRequiredForVerifyingType decorator](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-app-reference/extension-contracts/connected-fields/#get-type-definitions) in your data model. To check this, run the [extension test](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/test/integration-tests/#run-an-extension-test) for the [Get Type Definitions](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-app-reference/extension-contracts/connected-fields/#get-type-definitions) action and review the response. If the decorator is present for a property that should be optional, remove it from the data model.
- The property is not defined as optional in the data model. By default, Docusign treats properties as required if they are not explicitly defined as optional. Add an `isOptional` property set to `true` in the Concerto definition.

title: Troubleshooting for connected fields extensions
text: After populating a value in one or more connected fields, a **Verification failed** error is displayed instead of the expected verification result and message from the external system.

title: Troubleshooting for connected fields extensions
text:
- Your extension app registration is not configured with the correct parameters to connect to the external service. Run the Developer Console [extension tests](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/test/integration-tests/#run-an-extension-test) for the [Get Type Names](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-app-reference/extension-contracts/connected-fields/#get-type-names), [Get Type Definitions](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-app-reference/extension-contracts/connected-fields/#get-type-definitions), and [Verify](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-app-reference/extension-contracts/connected-fields/#verify) actions, and then check for any errors accessing the external service.
- Docusign does not have an up-to-date data model for your system of record. [Refresh the connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=qkv1716320270335.html) for the extension app in the Docusign App Center to update the data model information that Docusign stores. See [Connection behaviors and best practices](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/best-practices/connection-behaviors-and-best-practices/) for more information.
- The connection to the external service was invalidated due to authorization issues or deleted. [Re-establish](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=zsb1714159452358.html) the connection for the extension app in the Docusign App Center. See [Connection behaviors and best practices](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/best-practices/connection-behaviors-and-best-practices/) for more information.
- If envelopes are being created programmatically via API requests, the connected field values in the [Envelopes:create](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/reference/envelopes/envelopes/create/) request’s `extensionData` objects may be outdated or incorrect. Send a [TabInfo:GetConnectedFieldsTabGroups](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/connected-fields-api/reference/connectedfields/tabinfo/getconnectedfieldstabgroups/) request, and make sure the required values from the response are included in the `Envelopes:create` request. See [Connected fields concepts](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/connected-fields-api/concepts/) for more information.
- During template or envelope preparation, only optional connected fields were added to the document. Verification fails if a document does not contain any required connected fields. In the eSignature [Add Fields view](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=odn1578456409392.html), be sure to add required connected fields (marked with an asterisk) to the document, and then send a new envelope.

title: Troubleshooting for connected fields extensions
text: The signing view allows users to enter non-permitted values in a connected field, such as letters in a field that’s intended for numeric values.

title: Troubleshooting for connected fields extensions
text: The connected field did not have any data validation applied after it was added to the template or envelope in the eSignature [Add Fields view](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=odn1578456409392.html). Most Concerto properties appear as text fields in the Add Fields view. See [Transform logic](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/supported-extensions/data-model/#transform-logic) for details. To require signers to enter specific types of values, such as numeric values, use the [data validation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=jgd1578456357365.html) feature after adding connected fields to a template or envelope.

title: Troubleshooting for connected fields extensions
text: When trying to add or refresh a connection, the [connection management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=xyw1716318604389.html) page displays a **Data Error** banner.

title: Troubleshooting for connected fields extensions
text: Adding or refreshing a connection causes Docusign to execute [Get Type Names](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-app-reference/extension-contracts/connected-fields/#get-type-names) and [Get Type Definitions](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-app-reference/extension-contracts/connected-fields/#get-type-definitions) requests, but Docusign was unable to process both responses, possibly due to an error in the data model. To check this, run the [extension tests](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/test/integration-tests/#run-an-extension-test) for these two actions. You can use the [Concerto VSCode extension](https://concerto.accordproject.org/docs/tools/vscode/) to validate your data model and check for errors.

title: Troubleshooting for connected fields extensions
text:
- Get details about [connected fields requests and responses](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-app-reference/extension-contracts/connected-fields/).
- Find out more about [functional tests](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/test/functional-tests/).
- Learn how to run [integration tests](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/test/integration-tests/).

title: Review extension app logs
text:
The Developer Console All Logs feature enables you to review details about installs and uninstalls of an extension app. If you request assistance with an installation issue in the [Docusign Developer Community](https://community.docusign.com/developer-59), a Docusign support representative may ask you to provide details from the log.

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg height='845' width='1738' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' version='1.1'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

![Developer Console logs page](https://images.ctfassets.net/aj9z008chlq0/6mwa5Obqx06VyzVU6DBpTN/2cf7c5d1aac6e9350f6ffe78ae2b1473/AllLogs.png?w=1738&h=845&q=50&fm=png)

title: Review extension app logs
text:
To access an extension app’s logs:

1. Log in to the [Developer Console](https://devconsole.docusign.com).
2. On the **My Extension Apps** page, locate the row for the extension app you want to update.
3. Select the app to open the **Overview** page.
4. In the left navigation, select **All Logs**.

title: Review extension app logs
text:
You can filter the logs by date or environment. By default, logs are loaded for the last 30 days for the demo environment.

To change the date range:

1. Select the date filter.
2. Select one of these options and select **Apply**:
   - **Last 30 days**
   - **Last 6 month**
   - **Last year**
3. You can also select **Custom Date Range**, specify a range using one of these options, and select **Apply**:
   - **Between:** Define a start date and end date. Results include activity on the start and end dates, as well as between them.
   - **Before:** Select a date to return data from activity on and before that date.
   - **After:** Select a date to return data from activity on and after that date.

The page reloads and the content reflects the selected date range.

To change the environment:

1. Select the environment filter.
2. Select one of these options and then select **Apply**:
   - **Demo**
   - **Production**

The page reloads and the content reflects from the selected environment. See [Developer and production environments](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/publish/#developer-and-production-environments) for details about the difference between these environments.

title: Review extension app logs
text:
To review log details, select **View All** on the **Install and Uninstall Logs** tile. The list of logs loads. Each row in the list represents an installation or uninstallation of the extension app. You can [apply filters](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/troubleshooting/review-logs/#apply-log-filters) to limit the number of results.

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg height='797' width='1825' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' version='1.1'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

![Log detail page](https://images.ctfassets.net/aj9z008chlq0/4pkkE05NcuDKeSuiCc1UDL/4054f432927aa292971fdbd99d0a3ded/LogsPage.png?w=1825&h=797&q=50&fm=png)

To view details about an installation or uninstallation, select **View Details**. The following information is displayed:

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg height='619' width='800' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' version='1.1'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

![Log details](https://images.ctfassets.net/aj9z008chlq0/3K3Z39kTkV4eyXIRcrFhoE/c51344929e9c5d6c78138f646cad9980/LogDetails.png?w=800&h=619&q=50&fm=png)

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Timestamp (UTC) | The date and time at which the install or uninstall process finished. |
| App Instance ID | A unique ID representing the instance of your extension app that was installed or uninstalled. |
| App ID | A unique system-generated identifier for the extension app. |
| App Name | The unique name defined in the extension app’s [Basic information](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/register/use-form/app-center-listing/#update-basic-information) and in the extension app manifest [name](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-app-reference/app-manifest-reference/) property. |
| App Version | A unique ID representing the [version](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/versioning/) of the extension app. |
| Account ID | The Docusign account to or from which the extension app was installed or uninstalled. |
| Extension Type | A list of [extensions](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/supported-extensions/) that the extension app implements. |
| Status | `success` if the install or uninstall succeeded, or `failed` if it did not succeed. |
| Duration (MS) | The length of time in milliseconds for the install or uninstall process. |
| Debug ID (Trace Token) | A unique ID that Docusign can use to troubleshoot the cause of a failure. |
| Status Message | One of the following:  - `Created app instance: [app instance ID]` if an installation succeeded - `Delete app instance [app instance ID]` if an uninstall succeeded |

Select the **Download CSV** button to download a CSV file that contains the following information for each log file listed:

- Timestamp
- Status (`success` or `failed`)
- Duration in milliseconds of the installation or uninstallation process
- Debug ID
- Status message

title: Use extensions in Workflow Builder workflows
heading_path: Use extensions in Workflow Builder workflows
text:
The Workflow Builder API (and the [Workflow Designer](https://apps-d.docusign.com/send/workflows/) UI that it supports) enables customers to build and digitize their agreement processes, called *workflows*, with Docusign and Partner services. Customer process builders can use Workflow Builder workflows to easily build and deploy workflows that streamline their agreement process with clicks rather than code, as shown in the image.

You can use workflows to integrate Docusign first-party capabilities with external applications (called via extension apps) for a frictionless experience, enabling your customers to self-serve, discover, and enable new functionality using the [Docusign App Center](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center).

For details, see [Workflow Builder API 101 overview](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/workflow-builder-api/workflow-builder101/).

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg height='1453' width='828' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' version='1.1'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

![Image showing an example Maestro API workflow](https://images.ctfassets.net/aj9z008chlq0/6aV4rPYUa3mtChAJo4IlWv/86a8b8e39fbda2fb2aa6e4939360dcf8/WorkflowAllShort.png?w=828&h=1453&q=50&fm=png)

title: Customize and extend your agreement processes
text: Docusign IAM | App Center

title: Customize and extend your agreement processes
heading_path: Customize and extend your agreement processes
text:
Connect the tools of your trade within Docusign to make everything faster, easier and more automated. Discover and install partner apps in Docusign App Center.

App Center includes a variety of apps for both IAM and CLM plans.

[Explore App Center](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center "Explore App Center")[View IAM Plans](https://ecom.docusign.com/plans-and-pricing/iam "View IAM Plans")

title: Use Approved Apps
text:
Learn about Docusign approved extension apps and who can install and use.

Approved extension apps are a type of Docusign Packaged app that is available for use by all users in your organization.

title: Use Approved Apps
text:
The following types of extension apps are available on the Docusign App Center:

- Packaged
  - Docusign built apps using Docusign user credentials with a partner system
  - Require no user authentication
  - Example: Smarty: Address entry and verification
- Partner
  - Docusign partner-built app
  - Requires user authentication with the partner system
  - Example: Stripe: Accept online payments

title: Use Approved Apps
text:
You can manually approve any App Center app. If you are an administrator for an
account that already accesses IAM plans that include Docusign-built Data Verification and File Storage apps, these apps are automatically
approved.

To remove approval for these and any other apps, see [Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=oct1747682633917.html&_LANG=enus "As a Docusign App Center administrator, learn how to access, view, and manage Docusign approved apps.").

title: Use Approved Apps
text:
Previously, only Docusign Admins could install extension apps. App installation is required before any organization user in a Docusign account can use the app.

Now, upon purchasing a Docusign plan that includes packaged apps, all Docusign users can install and use approved apps in eSignature, Webforms, Workflow Builder, and Agreement Manager. Additionally:

- If you already administer an account that has access to an IAM plan that includes approved apps, those apps are automatically approved for your accounts.
- If you add IAM plans to either a new or existing account, these apps are approved for your account within ten minutes.

In these cases, you receive an in-product and email notification in the product alerting you that these apps are available for account users.

Docusign Account administrators can:

- View the approved apps page, including all approved apps and apps available for approval
- Install any app included in your plan, regardless of its pre-approval status
- Approve new apps
- Manage approved app settings
  - Permit users to install apps
  - View an approved app's detail page
  - Remove an app from approval

    Note: Existing users can continue to use the app. To restrict all access to an app, uninstall the app.
  - Uninstall apps
- Reinstall apps

  Docusign Workflow Builder workflows and eSignature templates are affected when you uninstall and reinstall apps and their connections.

  - If you uninstall and reinstall an app, you must reinstall and reconfigure the eSignature template or the app step in the Workflow Builder workflow.
  - If you delete an app's connection, you must create a new connection. Then, you must reconfigure the app step with this new connection. See the [Create Docusign App Connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=zsb1714159452358.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create Docusign apps connections for different user credentials in Docusign agreement workflows. You can create connections that are shared by all account users or used privately by the user who created the app connection.") article for details.
  - You can refresh an installed app's connection without affecting a workflow or template. See the [Reconnect a Docusign App Connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=qkv1716320270335.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Docusign app Reconnect a connection feature to reestablish the Docusign app’s connection to workflows.") article for details.

**Account (non-admin) users**  can:

- View apps approved for their use:
  - Use the Approved for you filter as described in Use the Approved for you filter to customize your apps in the [Locate Docusign Apps for Your Workflow Processes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ljy1744998611944.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to locate apps you can use in your workflow processes by using the Docusign Search feature and Works With and Industry filters.") article.
  - Visit the [Detail Page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=xkg1702568563800.html&_LANG=enus "Use a Docusign app's detail page to view important information such as an app summary, features, permissions, and resources.") for an approved app and see that it is approved available for to install
- Install apps
- Manage app connections

title: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 3: Update the System Property
heading_path: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 3: Update the System Property
text:
Learn how to update the system property in ServiceNow.

Exchange data between Docusign and ServiceNow with Docusign IAM for ServiceNow. Use the integration to kick off software and hardware
procurement requests and other downstream workflows.

This topic shows how to update the system property in ServiceNow.

1. Select the All tab from the top menu. Enter
   sys\_properties.list in the search box.

   The System Properties page displays a list of
   search results.
2. Search for and open the record
   x\_dosi\_docusign\_in.table.filter. The record will open
   once you hit the Enterkey, as the desired table will
   **not**appear in the dropdown results.
3. In the Value field, provide a comma-separated list of
   all the tables that the integration can access. When you are done, select
   Update.

   ![A screenshot of the record for x_dosi_docusign_in.table.filter. The Value field is highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/servicenowsyspropsentertables.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MzMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.a5rIB2zUz1Xz8I39oAe0Xhc5v3D0tpB70Z7LLG8JXcE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the record for x_dosi_docusign_in.table.filter. The Value field is highlighted.")

   Add the names of your desired tables to the Value property to have them appear
   in the dropdown list.

You completed this task.

title: Create a Private Docusign App
text:
Learn how to create a private Custom private app for data verification , eSignature,
or other Docusign features. Private apps provides custom data verification
fields for your organization.

You a private Custom private app for data verification and for other Docusign features such as eSignature, or Workflow Builder. Private apps provides
custom fields for your organization.

Before you begin

Identify:

- Use cases
- Envelope templates
- Target system APIs, such as a verification service or an internal database

1. Create your extension app as described in the [Build an Extension App](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/) article.

   For information about using Connected fields extensions, see these
   articles:

   - [Connected Fields API](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/connected-fields-api/concepts/)
   - [Connected fields extension overview](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/supported-extensions/connected-fields/)
2. Evaluate your API proxy requirements as described in the [API proxy](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/it-infrastructure/api-proxy/) article.

   Determine if you need to build a proxy layer to transform data exchange between
   your Custom Data Verification extension app to the target system.
3. Define your Custom data models.

   Define structured sets of fields that represent your agreements and
   data structures. Docusign uses the open source data
   modeling language [Concerto](https://concerto.accordproject.org/).

   A custom model can represent agreements such as documents or forms,
   or your external system’s object model, or both.
4. Integrate the extension app.
   1. Define a connection in the extension app.

      Configure parameters to obtain authorization from your target system
      as described in the [Connections](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/concepts/connections/) article.
   2. Implement actions as described in the [Actions](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/concepts/actions/) article.
5. Submit and test your app.

   Submit the app for review as described in the [Publish an extension app to the Docusign App Center](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/build-an-extension-app/publish/) article.
6. Publish your app.

   Privately publish the app for select accounts as described in the [Choosing private distribution instead of
   public](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/choosing-private-distribution/) article.

title: Troubleshoot Installing the Docusign IAM for ServiceNow App
heading_path: Troubleshoot Installing the Docusign IAM for ServiceNow App
text:
Learn how to troubleshoot issues with installing Docusign IAM for
ServiceNow.

Exchange data
between Docusign and ServiceNow with Docusign
IAM for ServiceNow. Use the integration to kick off software and hardware
procurement requests and other downstream workflows.

This topic shows how to troubleshoot issues that you might run into while installing and
configuring the app in ServiceNow. If your configuration fails, try these steps:

1. Check the application scope.
2. Verify the OAuth credentials (Client ID and Client Secret).
3. Ensure that the user has the required roles.
4. Confirm that the table required for integration is present in the
   sys\_property.
5. If you do not see a new ServiceNow table after adding it, refresh your browser. Also clear any filters that are applied to the table list.
6. For issues on the Docusign App Center, disable pop-up
   blocking.

title: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 6: Create Access Controls
heading_path: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 6: Create Access Controls
text:
Learn how to create access controls so that Docusign can access
ServiceNow tables.

Exchange data between Docusign and ServiceNow with Docusign IAM for ServiceNow. Use the integration to kick off software and hardware
procurement requests and other downstream workflows.

This topic shows how to create access controls (read, create, write).

1. Elevate your role so that you can edit the access controls. In the same table to which you granted Docusign access in the
   previous step, select your profile icon > Elevate role.

   The Elevate role dialog box displays.
2. Check the box next to security\_admin and select
   Update.

   ![A screenshot of the Elevate role dialog box. The box next to security_admin is checked.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/servicenowelevaterole.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5NDIsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.eGVrPE6jiw7ESwFSc99bSE4nXHI5314TI3oWpyQGdY0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Elevate role dialog box. The box next to security_admin is checked.")

   You return to the table record. A New button now
   displays on the right side of the Access Controls tab.
3. Select the New button to create a new access
   control.

   The Access Control New record page
   displays.
4. In the Operation field, set the control type
   (read, create, or
   write.)
5. Under Requires Role, in the Role
   field, add the role
   x\_dosi\_docusign\_in.docusign\_scripted\_rest\_api\_access\_role
   and select Submit.

   You return to the table record.
6. Repeat the process of creating a new access control for each required access
   control (read, create,
   write).If you run into issues granting access permission, it is likely
   because of a mismatch in scope between thetarget scopeand
   target table. Ensure the target scope and target
   table have the same name to avoid this.

You completed this task.

title: Docusign Apps Configuration Information

title: Use Docusign App Center to Manage App Connections
heading_path: Use Docusign App Center to Manage App Connections
text:
Learn how Docusign has changed how you connect and manage apps. Use Docusign App Center to manage the connections that were previously managed
through DocusignAdmin Agreement Actions.

Docusign has streamlined how you connect and manage applications such
as GoogleDrive, OneDrive, Sharepoint, Box, and Dropbox. Now you use Docusign App Center to manage the Connected Apps functionality
for Agreement Actions. This transition is part of a
migration to Microsoft Azure.

This move gives admins a single, secure place to install, approve, and manage
all connected apps (now connections of an installed app). Admins no longer need to
configure connections separately in multiple locations. It simplifies setup, strengthens
governance, and ensures consistency across Docusign products like
eSignature, Workflow Builder, Agreement Manager, or CLM.

Agreement action rules continue to be managed within the Docusign Account Administration as described in the [Create an Agreement Rule](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=wqg1596767500566.html&_LANG=enus)﻿﻿ article.

**Administrators**

In the App Center, administrators can:

- Approve apps for non-admin users
- Install applications
- Create app connections
- Track apps and their connections

**Non-admin users**

Previously, in Agreement Actions, non-admin users couldn’t view or access their
available apps. By moving Connected Apps to App Center, they can now use installed apps
across Docusign features. Non-admin users no longer rely on an
administrator to install or reconfigure them every single time.

In the App Center, Non-admin users can:

- Install approved connected apps
- Create private app connections

title: Use Docusign App Center to Manage App Connections
heading_path: Use Docusign App Center to Manage App Connections
Experience impact by persona and Docusign plan
text:
This table shows how the change in managing connected apps impact admins and
non-admin users based on their Docusign plan.

| Persona | Plan | Feature | Actions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Admins | eSignature | Agreement Actions | - Approve apps - Install apps - Create Connections - Set up and use connections in Connection Rules |
| IAM | - Agreement Actions - CLM - eSignature - Workflow Builder - Agreement Manager |
| Non-admins | eSignature | None | - Install admin-approved apps - Create connections   No access to Connection Rules |
| IAM | - Workflow Builder - Agreement Manager | - Install admin-approved apps - Create connections - Use connections in Workflow Builder and Agreement Manager |

title: Docusign App Center Connected Apps Workflow
heading_path: Docusign App Center Connected Apps Workflow
text:
Learn about the new workflow for using Docusign App Center to manage
app connections that were previously managed through Docusign Account
Administration Agreement Actions.

Here is the new workflow for using Docusign App Center to
manage app connections that were previously managed through Docusign
Account Administration Agreement Actions.

1. Install the application in Docusign App Center, as
   described in [Install Apps in the Docusign App
   Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install Docusign extension apps in the Docusign App Center.").﻿

   This example shows the app detail page where you select  Install
   App.

   ![A screenshot of the Install App option on an app detail page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-det-install.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MjEsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.oXQ7TSLnY7FjjHZGznM8KjtoH560_G4Ra37NdQXP1Go&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Install App option on an app detail page.")
2. Create app connections for installed apps in Docusign
   App Center as described in the [Create Docusign App Connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=zsb1714159452358.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create Docusign apps connections for different user credentials in Docusign agreement workflows. You can create connections that are shared by all account users or used privately by the user who created the app connection.") article.

   1. On the app's detail page, select Manage > New Connection

      These connection types are available:

      - Shared: Created by an Admin. Any account user
        can view and use the connection.
      - Private: Created by an Admin or a Non-admin
        account user. Only the account user who created the connection can
        view and use the connection.

        The Select Account type page displays.

        ![A screenshot of the Connection Select Account Type page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-add-new-connection.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MjEsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.oXQ7TSLnY7FjjHZGznM8KjtoH560_G4Ra37NdQXP1Go&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Connection Select Account Type page.")
   2. From this page, complete the steps to create the app connection as described in the [Create Docusign App Connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=zsb1714159452358.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create Docusign apps connections for different user credentials in Docusign agreement workflows. You can create connections that are shared by all account users or used privately by the user who created the app connection.") article.
3. Create Agreement rules for your app connections in Docusign Account Administration as described in the [Create an Agreement Rule](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=wqg1596767500566.html&_LANG=enus)﻿﻿ article.

   ![A screenshot of the Agreement Rule actions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/app-actions-add-actions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MjEsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.oXQ7TSLnY7FjjHZGznM8KjtoH560_G4Ra37NdQXP1Go&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Agreement Rule actions.")

You have now created an agreement action for an app using Docusign App
Center.

title: Take Action on App Center Connection Notifications
heading_path: Take Action on App Center Connection Notifications
text:
Learn about App Center in-product notifications that alert you to app connection issues..

App Center in-product notifications alert you to app connection issues.

You can find these notifications in these App Center locations.

title: Take Action on App Center Connection Notifications
heading_path: Take Action on App Center Connection Notifications
text:
As shown in this example, a red dot displays on the Manage Apps tab or the Installed Apps tab when connections need your attention. Click the tab with a red dot to view and resolve issues.

![A screenshot of a sample App Center notification indicator.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-notify-both-reddots.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NjgsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.urmCZGnXE3237WsrxhuBCtzvKriPW-_R60g6-PmVXHk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample App Center notification indicator.")

After selecting the tab, select the red x on the app with issues, as shown in this example.

![A screenshot showing a sample connection error notification.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-notify-con-err-list.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NjgsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.urmCZGnXE3237WsrxhuBCtzvKriPW-_R60g6-PmVXHk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing a sample connection error notification.")

title: Take Action on App Center Connection Notifications
heading_path: Take Action on App Center Connection Notifications
text:
A message banner displays on the [app detail page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=xkg1702568563800.html&_LANG=enus "Use a Docusign app's detail page to view important information such as an app summary, features, permissions, and resources."). Select Reconnect Now to resolve the issue as shown in this example.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-notify-det-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NjgsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.urmCZGnXE3237WsrxhuBCtzvKriPW-_R60g6-PmVXHk&_LANG=enus)

title: Take Action on App Center Connection Notifications
heading_path: Take Action on App Center Connection Notifications
text:
On the Connections page, if a connection needs attention, a notification similar to this example displays.

![A screenshot of a sample App Center Connections page notifications.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-not-conn-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NjgsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.urmCZGnXE3237WsrxhuBCtzvKriPW-_R60g6-PmVXHk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample App Center Connections page notifications.")

When you receive an Invalid Connection notification, click Reconnect to reestablish the app’s connection to workflows.

title: Docusign Extension App Regional Availability

title: Use Docusign Workflow Templates in the App Center
heading_path: Use Docusign Workflow Templates in the App Center
text:
Learn how to select and initiate a Docusign Workflow Builder template
from the Docusign App Center page.

You can select and initiate a Docusign Workflow Builder
template from the App Center page.

Depending on your account privileges, you can:

- Docusign Admin: Access and use Docusign Workflow Builder workflow templates in the Docusign App Center.
- Non-admin user: View information about a workflow template.

  Note: You cannot initiate a workflow
  template. Contact your Docusign Admin to
  gain access to Workflow Builder Workflows.

For detailed information about workflows, see the ​[Creating Workflows ​​​](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=tco1697826811758.html&_LANG=enus)​
article.

title: Use Docusign FedRAMP-Certified Apps
text:
Learn how to identify FedRAMP-certified apps in the Docusign App Center.

When FedRAMP customers access the Docusign App Center, they must know which
apps are FedRAMP compliant. Using this information, customers can make informed decisions
about app installation and creating connections. Administrators and non-admin users can now
install and use FedRAMP compliant apps in Workflow Builder and Agreement Manager.

Currently, these FedRAMP-certified apps are available in the Docusign App Center:

- Coupa
- Email Verification
- Microsoft Dynamics 365
- SharePoint
- Salesforce
- Smarty
- SSN and FEIN Verification
- Twillio
- Vonage

The FedRAMP-certified badge identifies FedRAMP-certified apps in the Docusign App Center. The badge displays on the app’s tile in the App Center page or the app's
detail page as shown in this example.

![A screenshot of the FedRAMP-certified badge in the Docusign App Center.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-fedramp-tile-ex.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NDMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.7SgbVzug3i7RXcB8wVnO_7ALMEAaggdgEg9hjCOqyMU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the FedRAMP-certified badge in the Docusign App Center.")

title: Use Docusign FedRAMP-Certified Apps
text:
