a Docusign Workflow Builder workflow.

From the App Center page

Select FedRAMP-certified  from the left menu pane to display
FedRAMP-certified apps.

From the Extension Apps page

Select Extension Apps  > Certifications > FedRAMP-certified .

![A screenshot of selecting Docusign Fed-RAMP-certified apps. from the Certifications menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-new-fedramp-sel-certs-filter.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NDMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.7SgbVzug3i7RXcB8wVnO_7ALMEAaggdgEg9hjCOqyMU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of selecting Docusign Fed-RAMP-certified apps. from the Certifications menu.")

For Workflow Builder in-product discovery

When adding a new step
to a workflow, as described in the [Creating Workflows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=tco1697826811758.html&_LANG=enus) article, select one of these access options.

- Browse Apps option
  1. Select Add a step
  2. Scroll down to the Connected Apps section and select Browse
     Apps to display the Docusign App Center page.

     ![A screenshot showing browsing for Docusign apps from a Workflow Builder workflow step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-app-fedramp-maestro.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NDMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.7SgbVzug3i7RXcB8wVnO_7ALMEAaggdgEg9hjCOqyMU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing browsing for Docusign apps from a Workflow Builder workflow step.")
  3. Select FedRAMP-certified  from the Certifications menu to display the available apps.
- Menu bar option
  1. Select App Center from the Workflow page top menu bar.
     ![A screenshot of selecting Docusign App Center from a Workflow Builder workflow step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-fedramp-start-from-workflow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NDMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.7SgbVzug3i7RXcB8wVnO_7ALMEAaggdgEg9hjCOqyMU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of selecting Docusign App Center from a Workflow Builder workflow step.")
  2. Select Fed Ramp-certified  from the Certifications menu.

     ![A screenshot of sample FedRAMP-certified Docusign apps.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-fedramp-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NDMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.7SgbVzug3i7RXcB8wVnO_7ALMEAaggdgEg9hjCOqyMU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of sample FedRAMP-certified Docusign apps.")

title: Install Apps in the Docusign App Center
heading_path: Install Apps in the Docusign App Center
text:
Learn how to install Docusign extension apps in the Docusign App Center.

Your access level determines the types of apps you can install.

Docusign account administrators

You must be an account administrator to install and manage packaged and partner apps from l Docusign App Center. See the [Organization Administrators](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=god1583359152535.html&_LANG=enus) article to learn more about these permissions.

Non-admin users

If your organization has a Docusign plan that includes packaged apps, you can install these approved apps. See the [Use Approved Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=aak1747679824594.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about Docusign approved extension apps and who can install and use.") article for detailed information.

You install apps at the account level. View and change accounts by selecting your profile picture and selecting Switch Account.

![A screenshot of the switch account option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/switch-account.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE3OTMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.VyOQpM29oZ0pmdqVMCepra0tPIiih3HwJm2PMp-dSzI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the switch account option.")

l Docusign workflows and eSignature templates are affected when you uninstall and reinstall apps and their connections.

Important:

- - If you uninstall and reinstall, an app, you must reinstall and reconfigure the eSignature template or the app step in the workflow.
  - If you delete an app's connection, you must create a new connection and reconfigure the app step with this new connection. See the [Create Docusign App Connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=zsb1714159452358.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create Docusign apps connections for different user credentials in Docusign agreement workflows. You can create connections that are shared by all account users or used privately by the user who created the app connection.") article for details.
  - You can refresh an installed app's connection without deleting without affecting a workflow or template. See the [Reconnect a Docusign App Connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=qkv1716320270335.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Docusign app Reconnect a connection feature to reestablish the Docusign app’s connection to workflows.") article for details.
  - Reinstall any apps that were previously installed from the Docusign App Center. Due to version issues, backward compatibility is not currently possible
  - Reconnect all Workflow Builder fields after upgrading.

You can install Docusign App Center apps:

- - On the Docusign App Center home page: Select the app tile and select Install App.
- - From an app's detail page: Select  Install App from any detail page.

1. Access  [Docusign App Center](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center) to begin using extension apps in your Docusign agreement workflows.

   Here is an example of the Docusign App Center page.

   ![A screenshot of the Docusign App Center dashboard.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-dash.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE3OTMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.VyOQpM29oZ0pmdqVMCepra0tPIiih3HwJm2PMp-dSzI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign App Center dashboard.")
2. Select Login to log on to your account.

   - Note the following:

title: Install Apps in the Docusign App Center
heading_path: Install Apps in the Docusign App Center
text:
VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.VyOQpM29oZ0pmdqVMCepra0tPIiih3HwJm2PMp-dSzI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign App Center dashboard.")
2. Select Login to log on to your account.

   - Note the following:

     - Some apps do not require authentication or an account connection.
     - The Install App  option is grayed out If you are not authorized to use an app. A message banner provides the reasoning. See the [Docusign App Center Banner Messages and App Labels](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=map1704218548543.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the messages and availability app labels that you encounter when using Docusign App Center and the actions you should take.") to learn more.
3. Optionally refine your search as described in the [Locate Docusign Apps for Your Workflow Processes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ljy1744998611944.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to locate apps you can use in your workflow processes by using the Docusign Search feature and Works With and Industry filters.")  article.
4. Select an app to install.

   The app's detail page displays. From this page, you can:

   - Install and authorize the app to use with platform services.
   - Review the app's overview, requirements, and resources.
5. Select Install App.
6. At the prompt, select  Install and Authorize to connect the app.
7. Select Continue.
8. If the app requires authorization, select Connect Account to connect the app to your Docusign account.
9. If you have Administrator privileges, select Manage > Add New Connection and specify the App connection type. See the [Create Docusign App Connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=zsb1714159452358.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create Docusign apps connections for different user credentials in Docusign agreement workflows. You can create connections that are shared by all account users or used privately by the user who created the app connection.") article for detailed information.

   These connection types are available:

   - Shared: Created by an Admin. Any account user can view and use the connection.
   - Private: Created by an Admin or an account user. Only the account user who created the connection can view and use the connection.
10. Authorize the application for use in Docusign by entering your credentials for the app.
11. Select Continue.

    The app is now ready to use to customize and extend agreement processes across multiple Docusign product surfaces, including Docusign Workflow Builder, the workflow builder tool.
12. To use the app in a platform service, such as Workflow Builder, select Use this app.

    The platform service displays. For example, if you want to use the app in a workflow, the workflows page displays.

    To learn more about using workflows, see the [Workflow Builder Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=irb1736981148403.html&_LANG=enus) article.

title: Configure a Box Store Files Step in an Agreement Workflow
heading_path: Configure a Box Store Files Step in an Agreement Workflow
text:
Learn how to configure a Box
store
files step in a Docusign agreement workflow.

Use the Box
store
files step to save documents generated by Workflow Builder workflow steps.
Generally, you position your archive step at the end of your workflow to archive
completed documents.

Restriction: Do not use a Box
store
files step if your workflow uses an eSignature step with multiple
participants and your first signer is designated as an embedded signer.
In this scenario, the first signer goes directly to sign from a prior workflow step.
Documents are archived directly after the embedded signer finishes signing and would
not include the actions of any subsequent participants.

Before you begin
:   - You must be an account administrator or have Workflows: Create user permissions as described in the [Workflow Builder Roles and Permission Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) article.
    - The extension app must be [installed and authorized](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus) on your account.

1. Create a new workflow as described in the [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus) article.
2. Select Add a step >  Store files with Box >  Configure.
3. Select the file to archive and select Apply.
4. Specify where to archive the file:
   1. Select the connection.
   2. Select the site.
   3. Select a drive.
   4. (Optional) Select a folder on the drive by selecting Add
      Folder. All existing folders are listed with an option
      to create a new folder.
5. Specify a name for your archive. Use the text and variable controls to build
   your file name.

   ![A screenshot of the Box Archive naming dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/acarchivewhat.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4ODEsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.VuZcL6-lUeRrPRUOzlQDMHdSnj3V1Ku6yvjwAVuMNuo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Box Archive naming dialog box.")
6. Review the completed file path and select Apply.

Your Box
store
files step is configured.

title: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 1: Install the Docusign IAM for ServiceNow App
heading_path: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 1: Install the Docusign IAM for ServiceNow App
text:
Learn how to install the Docusign IAM app in ServiceNow.

Exchange data between Docusign and ServiceNow with Docusign IAM for ServiceNow. Use the integration to kick off software and hardware
procurement requests and other downstream workflows.

This topic shows how to install the app in ServiceNow.

1. If you don’t already have a ServiceNow instance, create an instance from the
   [ServiceNow developer site](http://developer.servicenow.com/).
2. Install the Docusign IAM App from the ServiceNow Store.
3. In the top menu, select the globe icon.

   A panel containing a list of scopes displays.
4. Change the application scope to Docusign IAM.

   ![A screenshot of the top menu in ServiceNow. The globe icon is expanded to options for the application scope.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/servicenowchangescope.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MjcsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.P5nPJaoXyw7562gP05Jde3-0mXOPbVr45G1egoXRW9E&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the top menu in ServiceNow. The globe icon is expanded to options for the application scope.")

You completed this task.

title: Create the ServiceNow App Writeback Step
heading_path: Create the ServiceNow App Writeback Step
text:
Learn how to create the ServiceNow writeback step to use a ServiceNow instance in
Docusign Workflow Builder workflows.

1. Create a new workflow as described in the [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus) article.
2. Select Add a step Writeback to ServiceNow.
3. Enter the Step name.
4. Enter the Connection name.
5. Select the ServiceNow object to write to and select
   Next.
6. Update selected fields and select Save.

   The following example shows a completed ServiceNow workflow step.

   ![A screenshot of a sample ServiceNow workflow step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/acsnwkflcomplete.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5NTksInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.m2-nh0ChF_Rh-MimsgP8ZQPkw6xz0a6q2fnzAIlBF5M&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample ServiceNow workflow step.")

You have configured the ServiceNow workflow step.

title: Configure a Workato Workflow in a Workflow Builder Agreement Flow
heading_path: Configure a Workato Workflow in a Workflow Builder Agreement Flow
text: Learn how to configure a Workato workflow in a Docusign Workflow Builder agreement flow.

title: Configure a Workato Workflow in a Workflow Builder Agreement Flow
heading_path: Configure a Workato Workflow in a Workflow Builder Agreement Flow
text:
- Install and authorize the Workato Extension app on your account. See the  [Install the Workato Extension App from
  the Docusign App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=kyh1739988794638&topicId=dih1739994835924.html&_LANG=enus)  topic for details.
- Enter a valid bearer token when connecting Docusign Workflow Builder with the Workato account. See the [Connect Docusign Workflow Builder with the Workato Account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=vnk1739994877491.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to connect Docusign Workflow Builder with the Workato account.") topic for details.
- Enter a valid API token when connecting Docusign Workflow Builder with the Workato account. See the [Connect Docusign Workflow Builder with the Workato Account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=vnk1739994877491.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to connect Docusign Workflow Builder with the Workato account.") topic for details.
- Ensure the connection between Docusign Workflow Builder and Workato account is successful. See the [Connect Docusign Workflow Builder with the Workato Account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=vnk1739994877491.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to connect Docusign Workflow Builder with the Workato account.") topic for details.
- Review the [Getting started](https://docs.workato.com/getting-started.html) section of the Workato documentation for information on Workato recipes.
- Create your Workato recipe as an API recipe with an API endpoint as the starting point. See the [API Recipes](https://docs.workato.com/api-mgmt/api-recipes/#api-recipes) section of the Workato Developer API documentation for details.
- Must be an account administrator or have **Workflows: Create user permissions** as described in the
  [Workflow Builder Roles and Permission
  Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus).
- Log in to your Docusign account.

Use this procedure to configure a Workato workflow in a Workflow Builder agreement flow.

Important: In this release, Docusign Workflow Builder only supports synchronous workflows available in the Workato application.

Procedure

1. Create a workflow as described in the [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus) topic.
2. Select Add a step > Trigger Workato workflows > Configure.

   The configuration panel for the Trigger Workato workflows step opens.
3. Choose a workflow:
   1. Select the required Workato workflow (for example, API-Create Employee in BambooHR) from the Workflow Name or ID  dropdown list.

      Note: The Workflow Name or ID dropdown list only displays the workflows associated with the API collection selected in step 4b of the [Obtain the Auth Token from Workato](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=tsk628e1bf4-013c-4f6d-b72e-c5c9349f5960.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to obtain the auth token from the Workato application.") procedure.
   2. Select Next.

   ![A screenshot to select the required Workato workflow.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ds-workato-select-workflow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4OTgsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.CwxOANdSrRKmLCYf3q35pWjdsSZulA9zL_wWnHnf_GI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot to select the required Workato workflow.")
4. Map the workflow variables to fields:
   1. Select the required fields (for example, First Name and Account) for the selected Workato workflow input.
   2. Select Apply.

title: Configure a Workato Workflow in a Workflow Builder Agreement Flow
heading_path: Configure a Workato Workflow in a Workflow Builder Agreement Flow
text:
equired Workato workflow.")
4. Map the workflow variables to fields:
   1. Select the required fields (for example, First Name and Account) for the selected Workato workflow input.
   2. Select Apply.

   ![A screenshot to add the required input variables.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/add_required_input_varaibles.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4OTgsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.CwxOANdSrRKmLCYf3q35pWjdsSZulA9zL_wWnHnf_GI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot to add the required input variables.")

You have created and configured a Workato workflow step in Docusign Workflow Builder. You are redirected to the workflow overview in Workflow Builder. You can add additional steps to the workflow (for example, **Collect Data with Web Forms**, **Show a Confirmation Screen**, and so on). You can save a draft version of the workflow or publish it based on your requirement.

title: Install Google Docs for Docusign CLM
heading_path: Install Google Docs for Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to install and connect the Google Docs for Docusign CLM
integration. Google Docs for Docusign CLM enables users to edit documents
from Docusign CLM in Google Docs.

Google Docs for Docusign CLM enables
users to open and edit documents from Docusign CLM. Documents from the Docusign CLM document library can be edited in Google Docs and saved
back to Docusign CLM.

Note:

- By default, Google Docs for Docusign CLM will be connected
  to all instances of Google Docs within your organization. Users have the
  option to set up private connections to their organizational personal Google
  Drive, and to switch between connections. For more information on switching
  connections in Google Docs for Docusign CLM, see [Switch Between
  Private and Shared Connections in Google Docs for Docusign
  CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=alb1642965087302&topicId=yfy1750701784689.html&_LANG=enus).
- You must be a [Docusign Administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=god1583359152535.html&_LANG=enus) to install and configure the Google Docs application for Docusign CLM.

1. Log on to Docusign as an administrator.
2. Navigate to the [Docusign App Center](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center) and select
   Google Docs.
3. On the details page for Google Docs, select
   Install App. When prompted, select
   Continue to authorize the installation of the
   application.

   Google Docs for
   Docusign
    CLM installs.
4. Select Connect Account.

   The Google Workspace login window displays.
5. When prompted, select a Google Workspace account, or log in to your Google
   Workspace.

   A window requesting permission to connect your Google Workspace account
   to the Google Docs for
   Docusign
    CLM application displays.
6. Select the checkbox for See, edit, create, and delete all of your
   Google Drive files.
7. Select Continue to sign into Docusign
   with the selected credentials. If you would like to use a different account,
   select Cancel.

   A window listing the permission that Docusign needs on
   Google Drive and Google Docs displays.
8. Select Allow to enable the necessary permissions for Docusign to access your Google Workspace.

   You have successfully installed and connected Google Docs for
   Docusign

   CLM.

heading_path: Install Google Docs for Docusign CLM
text:
[Enable Google Docs
for Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=alb1642965087302&topicId=ebg1714405070200.html&_LANG=enus)

title: Switch Between Docusign App Center Environments
heading_path: Switch Between Docusign App Center Environments
text:
Learn about easily switching from Docusign App Center Demo and
Production environments.

Use the Switch feature to move between Demo and Production environments without leaving the Docusign App Center. For example, you can install, view, and manage an
app in your Demo (trial sandbox) environment. After testing, you may want to install the
same app in your Production environment.

This example shows the Try in Demo Account option.

![A screenshot of the Try in Demo Account option to change to the Demo environment.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/acswitchdemo.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE3OTgsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.8jWY9vJpFbEVYb3n_64jCqzyIokDOh6xDQ2OOA3aQ04&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Try in Demo Account option to change to the Demo environment.")

To summarize:

Try in Demo Account
:   Leaves the App Center Production environment and switches to the Demo
    environment.

Try in Production Account
:   Leaves the App Center Demo environment and switches to the Production
    environment.

title: Configure a SharePoint Store Files Step in an Agreement Workflow
heading_path: Configure a SharePoint Store Files Step in an Agreement Workflow
text:
Learn how to configure a SharePoint
store
files step in a Docusign agreement workflow.

Use the SharePoint
store
files step to save documents generated by Workflow Builder steps.
Generally, you position your archive step at the end of your workflow to archive
completed documents. Whatever state a document is in when the archive step generates
is what is saved to the specified file location.

Restriction: Do not use a SharePoint
store
files step if your workflow uses an eSignature step with multiple
participants and your first signer is designated as an embedded signer.
In this scenario, the first signer goes directly to sign from a prior workflow step.
Documents are archived directly after the embedded signer finishes signing and would
not include the actions of any subsequent participants.

Before you begin
:   - You must be an account administrator or have Workflows: Create user permissions as described in the [Workflow Builder Roles and Permission Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) article.
    - The extension app must be [installed and authorized](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus) on your account.

1. Create a new workflow as described in the [Create
   a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus) article.
2. Select Add a step >  Store files with SharePoint >  Configure.
3. Select the file to archive and select Apply.

   ![A screenshot of the SharePoint app file selection dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/apspfile.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4OTMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.f0niZ7lCqYu_HElOp8D_f1vE9Yq4OmnqFoJplYihefI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the SharePoint app file selection dialog box.")
4. Specify where to archive the file:
   1. Select the connection.
   2. Select the site.
   3. Select a drive.
   4. (Optional) Select a folder on the drive by choosing Add
      Folder. All existing folders are listed, as well as an
      option to create a new folder.

   ![A screenshot of the SharePoint location selection dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/apspwhere.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4OTMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.f0niZ7lCqYu_HElOp8D_f1vE9Yq4OmnqFoJplYihefI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the SharePoint location selection dialog box.")
5. Specify a file name for your archive. Use the text and variable controls to
   build your file name.

   ![A screenshot of the SharePoint app archive name dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/apspnamearchive.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4OTMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.f0niZ7lCqYu_HElOp8D_f1vE9Yq4OmnqFoJplYihefI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the SharePoint app archive name dialog box.")
6. Review the completed file path and select Apply.

Your SharePoint
store
files step is configured.

title: Configure Docusign Apps in an Agreement Workflow
heading_path: Configure Docusign Apps in an Agreement Workflow
text:
You can integrate Docusign apps to automate your agreement workflows and boost productivity.

Add apps from Docusign App Center when creating agreement workflows in Docusign Workflow Builder. With Docusign apps, you can customize and automate your agreement processes as described in the [Creating Workflows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=tco1697826811758.html&_LANG=enus) article.

Additionally, you can use the Google Docs app with Docusign CLM as described in the  [Google Docs for Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=alb1642965087302&topicId=gcn1714403494382.html&_LANG=enus) article.

See the [Agreement Uploads and Ingestion ﻿](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rrs1712255668350.html&_LANG=enus) article to learn upload or ingest agreements in Agreement Manager.

title: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
text:
Learn to add and configure a Read from Salesforce step to import data from Salesforce records.

Before you begin
:   - Required: Installation of the Salesforce app from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account administrator.
    - Required: Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add steps like [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus), [web forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and more to gather recipients, documents, and merge fields for your workflow.

You can use a Read from Salesforce step to import data from
Salesforce records to your workflow.
Administrators link workflow fields to Salesforce fields in the Read from Salesforce
step. When task recipients open an envelope, they see Salesforce data imported from
the record. Importing Salesforce data improves efficiency and reduces errors by
eliminating manual data entry.

Example: You build a workflow with a Read from Salesforce step. In the Read from Salesforce step, you link to a Salesforce record's Amount field. When a workflow recipient opens a contract, they see the record's Amount field pre-filled.

Use this procedure to create a Read from Salesforce step in a workflow:

1. Add a Read from Salesforce step as a Docusign administrator:
   1. Go to Agreements > Workflows and select Edit next to a workflow name.

      The workflow editor page displays.
   2. Select the Add a step ![A screenshot displays a plus sign inside of a square.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_add_step_plus_sign.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTIsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.aSScXTnv6ME7qipkUwCxePjmPOPaBryrUYKeW9tkStY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a plus sign inside of a square.") icon.

      The Add New Step dialog box displays.
   3. Search for steps: Enter and select `Read from Salesforce`.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Read from Salesforce step added.
2. Name your Read from Salesforce step:
   1. Select Configure on the Read from Salesforce step.

      The configuration dialog box displays.
   2. Step Name: Keep or edit the name. It is helpful to add the name of the Salesforce object, like `Account - Read from Salesforce`.
3. Where will you read from?
   1. Connection: Select the field and choose a
      Salesforce organization that you connected to Docusign. Multiple options display if you connect more than one Salesforce
      organization.
   2. (Optional) Add New Connection: Select this button if you want to [connect more than one Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ogk1719256147008.html&_LANG=enus).
   3. (Optional) Manage Connections: Select this
      button if you want to [add, edit, refresh, or delete a
      Salesforce connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=uaw1719259450391.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. Salesforce object: Search for an object or choose one from the dropdown menu.
   5. Select Next.

title: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
text:
docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=uaw1719259450391.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. Salesforce object: Search for an object or choose one from the dropdown menu.
   5. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays a Read from Salesforce workflow step with the Connection and Salesforce object fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Where_will_you_read_from_object.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTIsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.aSScXTnv6ME7qipkUwCxePjmPOPaBryrUYKeW9tkStY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Read from Salesforce workflow step with the Connection and Salesforce object fields highlighted.")
4. Which fields would you like to read?: Select workflow
   fields to map to Salesforce fields. When workflows start, Docusign adds this data to agreements.
   1. Select Add or Remove Fields.

      The Add or Remove Fields page displays.
   2. Select the check box next to each Salesforce field that you'd like to
      use as a workflow variable. You'll be able to use these fields in later
      steps.

      Tip: When you select objects on the
      left, related objects open. The Source column
      displays each field's object path. ![A screen capture displays the Add or Remove Fields page with related objects highlighted and fields selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Add_or_Remove_Fields.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTIsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.aSScXTnv6ME7qipkUwCxePjmPOPaBryrUYKeW9tkStY&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the Add or Remove Fields page with related objects highlighted and fields selected.")
   3. Select Save and Exit.

      The Add or Remove Fields dialog box closes. The fields that you
      selected display.
   4. Select Next.
5. Which records are you reading from?: Create a rule that matches Salesforce fields with equivalent workflow fields. Workflows use this rule to read Salesforce data. You can create as many rules as you want:
   1. Salesforce field: Select a Salesforce field to match with a Docusign field.

      Note: When you select a (reference) type field, a new field opens below it. Choose a child object from the new field.
   2. Operator: Choose an option like Equal
      to, Less than, and so on.
   3. Workflow field: Select a Docusign workflow field to match with your Salesforce field:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new variable and select Save.

        Note: The name that you enter must exactly match an existing field. For example, the rule fails if you enter CompanyName for the Company\_Name variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

        Note: Steps like Read
        from HubSpot and Read from
        Salesforce display fields from up to five
        records. If more than five records are present, Salesforce
        logic decides which five to display. Selecting fields from
        multiple records helps you automate processes like mapping
        multiple Salesforce contacts to multiple Docusign participants.![A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Workflow_field_selections.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTIsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.aSScXTnv6ME7qipkUwCxePjmPOPaBryrUYKeW9tkStY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.")

title: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Read from Salesforce Step
text:
WduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTIsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.aSScXTnv6ME7qipkUwCxePjmPOPaBryrUYKeW9tkStY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.")

        You can use the fields that you select here in
        later steps like Collect Data with
        Webforms or Send Documents for
        Signature.
   4. Select Done to save your rule.

      ![A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_HubSpot_Read_Which_record_are_you_reading_from_Greater_than.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTIsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.aSScXTnv6ME7qipkUwCxePjmPOPaBryrUYKeW9tkStY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.")
6. (Optional) Add more rules:
   - +AND: Workflow Builder only imports Salesforce data that
     matches all of your rules.
   - +OR: Workflow Builder imports Salesforce data that matches
     any of your rules.
7. How many records should this step return?:
   - A single record: Returns the first record that
     matches your rule.
   - Multiple records: Returns up to five records that match your
     rule.

     Note: If more than five records are present, Salesforce logic
     decides which five to display.
8. (Optional) Preview this step: See examples of how this rule will work with up to ten Salesforce records:
   1. Select the Preview this step link.

      The Add Data to Preview Step page opens.
   2. Keep or edit the data provided in the field.
   3. Select Preview Salesforce Data.

      ![A screenshot displays the Read from Salesforce Add Data to Preview Step page of a Read from Salesforce workflow step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Read_from_Salesforce_Add_Data_to_Preview_Step.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTIsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.aSScXTnv6ME7qipkUwCxePjmPOPaBryrUYKeW9tkStY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Read from Salesforce Add Data to Preview Step page of a Read from Salesforce workflow step.")

      The page refreshes, displaying Salesforce data that your Read from Salesforce rule found.
   4. Select Close.

      The Add Data to Preview Step page closes.
   5. Select Apply.

   The Read from Salesforce dialog box closes. The workflow page displays your new step.

You have completed the process. You have built a Read from Salesforce step in your workflow.
When this step is triggered in a workflow, it imports field data from the Salesforce
records that you mapped.

title: Welcome to Docusign App Center
text:
Learn about using the Docusign App Center to discover and enable applications to extend your agreement workflows.

[Docusign App Center](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center) is a place to discover and install partner applications that extend Docusign product capabilities.

title: Welcome to Docusign App Center
heading_path: How extension apps work in Docusign
text:
Use extension apps to customize and extend agreement processes across multiple Docusign products, including Docusign Workflow Builder, the agreement workflow builder tool.

To learn more about Docusign Workflows, see the [Workflow Builder Overview](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=dnx1696972415150.html&_LANG=enus) article.

For a summary of App Center questions, see the  [Docusign App Center FAQ Overview](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gcc1750788193890&topicId=dvb1750792155723.html&_LANG=enus) article.

title: Welcome to Docusign App Center
text:
Before you can install the apps, make sure that you have the required permissions for both the Docusign account and the app you want to install.

Install and manage apps
:   You must be a Docusign account administrator with full administrative permissions to install and manage Docusign apps. See the [Permission Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus) article to learn more about these permissions.
:   You install apps at the account level. To change accounts, select Switch Account in the Profile menu.

Banner messages
:   Depending on permissions, banner messages display on app detail pages. See the  [Docusign App Center Banners and Messages and App Labels](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=map1704218548543.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the messages and availability app labels that you encounter when using Docusign App Center and the actions you should take.") article to learn more about their meaning.

App account restrictions
:   Currently, App Center only supports work and school accounts for extension apps. For example, App Center does not support personal accounts for Google Drive or SharePoint.

title: Welcome to Docusign App Center
text:
Organizations can globally access Docusign App Center.

Note:

- Along with U.S. English, Docusign App Center and Apps are available in the [Sender-supported languages](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=spo1578456459828.html).

  Note: Region-specific apps may not be supported in all sender-supported languages.
- Some apps are specific to certain regions. You may not see all apps if they're not deployed in your region. See the [App Center Regional Availability](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=stg1748631650074.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the regional availability for Docusign App Center apps and where to find this information in Docusign App Center.") article for information about supported regions and region designations that display on the app's App Detail page.

title: Welcome to Docusign App Center
text:
Use apps from the Docusign App Center within these Docusignfeatures:

- Workflow Builder

  Use Docusign Workflow Builder to build and deploy customizable, code-free agreement workflows with the appropriate user permissions assigned to you by your Docusign Administrator.

  See the [Workflow Builder Roles and Permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) article to learn more about Workflow Builder permissions.
- eSignature Data Verification

  Use the Data Verification capabilities with prebuilt, drag-and-drop components powered by trusted apps. Data Verification verifies information and helps decrease document errors. See the  [Welcome to Docusign Data Verification](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qki1698158012210&topicId=gxs1698158285710.html&_LANG=enus) article to learn about using Data Verification apps.
- CLM

  Use Google Docs for CLM to open documents in Google Docs for changes and revisions. Once you make changes in Google Docs, you can save the document to CLM. See the [Google Docs for CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=alb1642965087302&topicId=gcn1714403494382.html&_LANG=enus)  App article for instructions on configuring the Google Docs for CLM app.
- Agreement Manager

  Upload documents for changes and revisions in Docusign Agreement Manager. See the [File Ingestion from Third-Party Cloud Providers](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=byx1727376126901.html&_LANG=enus)  article to learn about using Google Drive with Agreement Manager.

title: Welcome to Docusign App Center
text:
As a developer, you can create, test, and publish apps to the Docusign App Center through the  [Docusign Developer Center](https://developers.docusign.com/).

Developers can review Docusign API documentation to learn how to build extension apps for the Docusign App Center. See the [Extension apps 101 overview](https://developers.docusign.com/extension-apps/extension-apps-101/) on the Docusign Developer Center.

For more information about building extension apps, access the Docusign University course [Build Extension Apps for Docusign App Center](https://dsucustomers.docusign.com/build-extension-apps-for-the-docusign-app-center).

title: Welcome to Docusign App Center
text:
The App Center community group provides information to customers who are actively participating in the beta program. Group members can ask questions, engage with other participants, and learn about updates throughout the program.

See the [App Center community page](https://community.docusign.com/docusign-app-center-82) for more information.

title: Configure a Read From Dynamics 365 Step in Agreement Workflows﻿
heading_path: Configure a Read From Dynamics 365 Step in Agreement Workflows﻿
text:
Learn to add and configure a Read from Dynamics 365 step to import data from Dynamics
365 records.

Before you begin
:   - Required: Installation of the Dynamics 365 app
      from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account administrator.
    - Required: Access to a suitable template to use for your workflow
      step. Review the [Template Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=xnw1709852160602.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.
    - Recommended: Open the Docusign template in another
      window to reference as you set up your step. This will help you
      correctly configure your Read from Dynamics 365 step.

You can use a Read from Dynamics 365 step to import data from
Dynamics 365 records to your Docusign workflow. Administrators
link workflow fields to Dynamics 365 fields in this step . When task
recipients open an envelope, they see Dynamics 365 data imported from the record.
Importing Dynamics 365 data improves efficiency and reduces errors by eliminating
manual data entry.

To learn about installing, configuring, and integrating Dynamics 365 with agreement
workflows, see the [Use Docusign
Workflow Builder with Microsoft Dynamics 365](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pic1741250291019&topicId=lkn1741250360427.html&_LANG=enus) article.

Example: You build a workflow with these steps:
Collect Data with Web Forms, Read from Dynamics
365, and Writeback to Dynamics 365 step. In the
writeback step, you link the Dynamics 365 opportunity Budget
Amount field to the web form's Budget Amount
field. A workflow recipient opens a contract and sees the opportunity's Budget
Amount field pre-filled. The recipient changes the budget amount and Docusign updates the Budget Amount field on the Dynamics 365
record.

Note: To read data from child or related records, first create an
Account-based step.  [Next, create
Read from Dynamics 365 steps based on child objects](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pic1741250291019&topicId=ahc1741673652580.html&_LANG=enus) .

Use the following procedure to create a Read from Dynamics 365
step in a Docusign workflow:

title: Configure a Read From Dynamics 365 Step in Agreement Workflows﻿
heading_path: Configure a Read From Dynamics 365 Step in Agreement Workflows﻿
text:
/support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pic1741250291019&topicId=ahc1741673652580.html&_LANG=enus) .

Use the following procedure to create a Read from Dynamics 365
step in a Docusign workflow:

1. Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add steps like [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus), [web
   forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and more to gather recipients, documents, and merge fields for your workflow.
2. Select Add a step  >  Read from Dynamics 365  >  Configure.
3. Step Name: Keep or edit the name. It is helpful to add
   the name of the Dynamics 365 object, like Account - Read from
   Dynamics 365.
4. Where will you read from?
   1. Connection: Choose a Dynamics 365 organization
      you connected to Workflow Builder.
   2. Dynamics 365 object: Use the search field or
      dropdown menu to select an object.
   3. Select Next.
5. Which fields would you like to read?: Select workflow
   fields to map to Dynamics 365 fields. When workflow recipients open contracts,
   they see data Docusign imported from a record.
   1. Select Add or Remove Fields.
   2. Select Dynamics 365 fields that you'd like to use as workflow
      variables. You'll be able to use them in later steps.
   3. Select Save and Exit.

   The Select Fields dialog box closes. The fields you selected
   display.
6. Which record are you reading from?: Create up to five
   rules to read from Dynamics 365 records:
   1. Select Add rule.
   2. IF:

      - Any Rules: Select to choose a Dynamics
        365 record matching any rules.
      - All Rules: Only choose a record that
        matches all rules.
   3. Dynamics 365 field: Select a Dynamics 365 field
      to match with a Docusign workflow field.
   4. Workflow field: Select a Docusign workflow field to match with a Dynamics 365
      field.

      - Starting Variables: Find custom variables
        created outside of the workflow steps.
      - WORKFLOW STEPS: Find variables from other
        workflow steps.
   5. Select Save and Exit.

      ![A screen capture demonstrates adding a rule to a Read from Dynamics 365 workflow step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/configure_a_read_step.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MDIsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.Tb9QiKlsWWWdQ3z5xiaGQpcqoxXvKB6JXuDvekF3oQI&_LANG=enus "A screen capture demonstrates adding a rule to a Read from Dynamics 365 workflow step.")
   6. Select Next.
7. Select Apply.

   The Read From Dynamics 365 column closes. The workflow page
   displays your new step.

You have completed the process. You have built a Read from
Dynamics 365 step in your workflow. When this step is triggered in a workflow,
it imports field data from the Dynamics 365 records that you mapped.

title: Administrator: Work with Approved Apps
text:
As a Docusign App Center administrator, you make extension apps

As a Docusign App Center administrator, you make extension apps available for
