title: Configure a Writeback to Dynamics 365 Step in an Agreement Workflow
heading_path: Configure a Writeback to Dynamics 365 Step in an Agreement Workflow
text:
Learn to add and configure a Writeback to Dynamics 365 step to update Dynamics 365 records.

Before you begin
:   - Required: Installation of the Dynamics 365 app from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account administrator.
    - Required: Access to a suitable template to use for your workflow step. Review the [Template Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=xnw1709852160602.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.
    - Required: [Connect Docusign Workflow Builder with the Microsoft Dynamics 365 Account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=och1741251029395.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to establish a connection between Docusign Workflow Builder and the Microsoft Dynamics 365 account.").
    - Recommended: Open the template in another window to view as you set up your step. This helps you to correctly configure your Writeback to Dynamics 365 step.

You can use a Writeback to Dynamics 365 step to update Dynamics 365 records with information from your Docusign workflow. This step updates Dynamics 365 fields, creates new records, and more. Administrators link workflow fields to Dynamics 365 fields in this step. When task recipients enter data in workflow fields, Docusign updates the information in the linked Dynamics 365 record fields. Writeback improves efficiency and reduces errors by removing the need to manually update records.

To learn about installing, configuring, and integrating Dynamics 365 with agreement workflows, see the [Use Docusign Workflow Builder with Microsoft Dynamics 365](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pic1741250291019&topicId=lkn1741250360427.html&_LANG=enus) article.

Example: You can build a workflow that includes a Collect Data with Web Forms step and a Writeback to Dynamics 365 step. In the writeback step, you link the Dynamics 365 opportunity's Budget Amount field to the web form's Budget Amount field. A workflow recipient updates and signs a contract for an existing opportunity named Fontara. The recipient updates the Fontara contract's Amount field from $20,000 to $25,000. When the recipient completes the assignment, Docusign updates the Fontara record's Amount field to $25,000.

Note: Follow the required formatting for all date fields. See the [Format Date Fields on a Writeback to Dynamic](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pic1741250291019&topicId=dfz1742231023702.html&_LANG=enus) article for more information.

Use the following procedure to create a Writeback to Dynamics 365 step in a Docusign workflow:

title: Configure a Writeback to Dynamics 365 Step in an Agreement Workflow
heading_path: Configure a Writeback to Dynamics 365 Step in an Agreement Workflow
text:
t-item?bundleId=pic1741250291019&topicId=dfz1742231023702.html&_LANG=enus) article for more information.

Use the following procedure to create a Writeback to Dynamics 365 step in a Docusign workflow:

1. Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add steps like [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus), [web forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and more to gather recipients, documents, and merge fields for your workflow.
2. Select Add a step  >  Writeback to Dynamics 365  >  Configure.
3. Step Name: Keep or edit the name. It is helpful to add the name of the Dynamics 365 object, like Account - Writeback to Dynamics 365.
4. Where will you write to?
   1. Connection: Choose a Dynamics 365 organization connected to Workflow Builder.
   2. Dynamics 365 object: Select an object from the dropdown menu.
   3. Write settings: Select an option from the list that opens:

      - Update or Create: Update a record or create a new record if none exists.
      - Update: Update an existing record (no required fields): Updating a record that exists
      - Create: Create a new record (required fields) New account sign-up flow or onboarding
   4. Select Next.
5. Which fields are you writing to?: Select workflow fields to map to Dynamics 365 fields. When the workflow recipients complete tasks, the data they enter updates field data on Dynamics 365 records.
   1. (Optional) Add or Remove Fields: Choose more fields and select Save and Exit.
   2. Select the Select field below a Dynamics 365 field name.
   3. Choose an existing workflow field or create a new field:

      - Starting Variables: Find custom variables created outside of the workflow steps.
      - WORKFLOW STEPS: Find variables from other workflow steps.![A screenshot displays matching workflow fields to Dynamics 365 fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/matching-workflow-fields-to-Dynamics-365-fields_writeback.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MDUsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.aYTwy4mO2L3VlMRlEmstBcr7F8RU09CeezMXHNQQClc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays matching workflow fields to Dynamics 365 fields.")
      - (Optional) Overwrite if field value exists: Select this check box if you want the data entered by a workflow participant to overwrite existing Dynamics 365 data.
6. Which record are you writing to? (optional):  [Build a rule to writeback to existing Dynamics 365 records](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pic1741250291019&topicId=ykg1741635819126.html&_LANG=enus). If you do not add a rule, the writeback step creates a new Dynamics 365 record.
7. Select Apply.

You have completed the process. You have built a Writeback to Dynamics 365 step in your workflow. When this step is triggered in a workflow, it updates field data on Dynamics 365 records you mapped. Users do not need to manually update records.

title: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 2: Generate a New Client Secret for OAuth
heading_path: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 2: Generate a New Client Secret for OAuth
text:
Learn how to create a new client secret for Docusign IAM for ServiceNow.

Exchange data between Docusign and ServiceNow with Docusign IAM for ServiceNow. Use the integration to kick off software and hardware procurement requests and other downstream workflows.

This topic shows how to create a new client secret for OAuth.

1. In App Engine Studio, select the All tab.

   A new panel with a search box opens.
2. Search for OAuth. Select Application Registry from the results.

   ![A screenshot of the All panel. It shows a search for Oath. The result named Application Registry is highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/servicenowoauth.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MzAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.eK4aE3frQYCBheLNuEdRuJWJtJ2Iw7SHv7t-WMZh-eQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the All panel. It shows a search for Oath. The result named Application Registry is highlighted.")

   The Application Registry window displays.
3. Select Docusign IAM OAuth from the list.

   The details page for the application displays.
4. Unlock the field by selecting the lock icon, then replace the existing Client Secret with a new one. Once finished, lock the field.
5. Set the Redirect URL for your region.
   - Demo: https://demo.services.docusign.net/act-gateway/v1.0/oauth/callback

     Note: You can use the demo redirect URL irrespective of your region.
   - Production (U.S.): https://us.services.docusign.net/act-gateway/v1.0/oauth/callback
   - Production (CA): https://ca.services.docusign.net/act-gateway/v1.0/oauth/callback
   - Production (AU): https://au.services.docusign.net/act-gateway/v1.0/oauth/callback
   - Production (EU): https://eu.services.docusign.net/act-gateway/v1.0/oauth/callback
   - Production (JP): https://jp.services.docusign.net/act-gateway/v1.0/oauth/callback
6. Make a note of the Client ID, Client Secret, and ServiceNow instance name. You must enter them on the Docusign platform later. You can find the ServiceNow instance name at the beginning of the URL in your browser’s address bar. When you are done and you have the details that you need, select Update at the top right.

   ![A screenshot of the details page for the application. The Client Secret field is highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/servicenowregistrydetails.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MzAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.eK4aE3frQYCBheLNuEdRuJWJtJ2Iw7SHv7t-WMZh-eQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the details page for the application. The Client Secret field is highlighted.")

You return to the Application Registry window. You now have the Client ID, Client Secret, and ServiceNow instance name. You need these to authorize the ServiceNow app when you [Create the ServiceNow App Writeback Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ejl1711393084084.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create the ServiceNow writeback step to use a ServiceNow instance in Docusign Workflow Builder workflows.").

title: Locate Docusign Apps for Your Workflow Processes
heading_path: Locate Docusign Apps for Your Workflow Processes
text:
Learn how to locate apps you can use in your workflow processes by using the Docusign Search feature and Works With and Industry filters.

You can locate extension apps you can use in your workflow processes using the Docusign App Center.

You can also find available  [Workflow Builder templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=con8f35c78f-56b7-466c-91e6-c0ea7d03c516.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to select and initiate a Docusign Workflow Builder template from the Docusign App Center page.").

To use the Search feature:

1. Go to the App Center as described in the [Access Docusign Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=bkt1740757069317.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to browse for and connect to Docusign extension apps from various Docusign feature locations.")
   article.
2. Enter the name in the Search field.

   ![A screenshot of the Docusign App Center search bar.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-new-search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE3ODcsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.rN0RZc9iGQ810KrrJoNbZRwFkKvqmnDFK_DmvsiSA5E&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign App Center search bar.")

   Any apps corresponding to your entry display.

   ![A screenshot showing the results of the Docusign App Center Search feature.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/app-center-search-features.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE3ODcsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.rN0RZc9iGQ810KrrJoNbZRwFkKvqmnDFK_DmvsiSA5E&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing the results of the Docusign App Center Search feature.")
3. Select the app that you want.

   The app's Detail Listing page displays.
4. Install the app as described in the [Install Apps in the App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install Docusign extension apps in the Docusign App Center.") article.

title: Locate Docusign Apps for Your Workflow Processes
heading_path: Locate Docusign Apps for Your Workflow Processes
Use filters to refine your apps search
text:
1. Go to the App Center as described in the [Access Docusign Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=bkt1740757069317.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to browse for and connect to Docusign extension apps from various Docusign feature locations.") article.
2. Select the Works With or Industry
   filter to display a list of Docusign products as shown in this
   Works With filter example.

   ![A screenshot showing the Docusign App Center Works with filter.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-workswith.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE3ODcsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.rN0RZc9iGQ810KrrJoNbZRwFkKvqmnDFK_DmvsiSA5E&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing the Docusign App Center Works with filter.")
3. Select one or more Docusign products from the list.
   - Select Apply to display apps that work with
     your product.
   - Select Clear to start over.
4. Select from the resulting apps that work with the selected Docusign products.

   ![A screenshot showing the resulting apps for the Works With Agreement Manager Filter entry.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-works-with-result-pg.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE3ODcsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.rN0RZc9iGQ810KrrJoNbZRwFkKvqmnDFK_DmvsiSA5E&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing the resulting apps for the Works With Agreement Manager Filter entry.")
5. Select the app that you want.

   The app's Detail Listing page displays.
6. Select one of these options:

   - Install the app as described in the [Install Apps in the App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install Docusign extension apps in the Docusign App Center.")
     article.
   - If the app is already installed, select Use This
     App.

title: Locate Docusign Apps for Your Workflow Processes
heading_path: Locate Docusign Apps for Your Workflow Processes
Use the Approved for you filter to customize your apps search
text:
1. Go to the App Center as described in the [Access Docusign Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=bkt1740757069317.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to browse for and connect to Docusign extension apps from various Docusign feature locations.") article.
2. Select the Approved for you filter and select one of more of these apps.

   ![A screenshot of the App Center Approved for you filter options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/approved-for-you-filter.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE3ODcsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.rN0RZc9iGQ810KrrJoNbZRwFkKvqmnDFK_DmvsiSA5E&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the App Center Approved for you filter options.")

   - Approved for you filter: Displays apps your
     Admin has approved for your organization. See the [Use Approved Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=aak1747679824594.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about Docusign approved extension apps and who can install and use.") topic for
     details about using these apps.
     - Administrator approved: All Docusign users can install and use these
       approved apps in eSignature feature and Webforms.
     - Private apps: Custom apps created
       exclusively by and for your organization.

title: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 8: Install the ServiceNow App From the Docusign App Center
heading_path: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 8: Install the ServiceNow App From the Docusign App Center
text:
Learn how to install the ServiceNow app for use with Docusign Workflow Builder.

Exchange data between Docusign and ServiceNow with Docusign IAM for ServiceNow. Use the integration to kick off software and hardware procurement requests and other downstream workflows.

This topic shows how to install the ServiceNow app from the Docusign App Center. This is the last step required to use the integration with Docusign Workflow Builder.

1. Follow the steps in the article [Install Apps in Docusign App Center﻿](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install Docusign extension apps in the Docusign App Center.").
2. In the Connect Account step at the end of the installation process, provide the **Client ID**,**Client Secret**, and **ServiceNow Instance Name** from the previous steps.If the new connection doesn't automatically load in a pop-up window, click the refresh button and it should appear.
3. Select Save.

You completed this task, and all the tasks required to set up your ServiceNow instance for use with Docusign Workflow Builder.

title: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 8: Install the ServiceNow App From the Docusign App Center
heading_path: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 8: Install the ServiceNow App From the Docusign App Center
text:
- If you run into any issues, see [Troubleshoot Installing the Docusign IAM for ServiceNow App](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=bur1727290839878.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to troubleshoot issues with installing Docusign IAM for ServiceNow.").
- If the installation was successful, move to [Create the ServiceNow App Writeback Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ejl1711393084084.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create the ServiceNow writeback step to use a ServiceNow instance in Docusign Workflow Builder workflows.").

title: Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps
heading_path: Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps
text:
As a Docusign App Center administrator, learn how to access, view, and manage Docusign approved apps.

As a Docusign App Center administrator, you can access, view, and manage Docusign approved apps.

1. From the  [Docusign App Center page](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center), select Extension Apps > Approved Apps.

   Tip: You can alternately select Manage > Approved Apps.

   The List of approved apps section shows apps that are available for your organization's users.

   ![A screenshot of the App Center Approved Apps page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-approved-pg.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MzgsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.NDWEfpPnRIoRvbCuHZte_dphqiwCSJDM8z-toB5OpPY&_LANG=enus)
2. For apps that are approved but not installed, select  Install App to install the app for all account users.
3. Use the overflow menu to manage your approved apps.

   | Function | Select to: |
   | --- | --- |
   | View Details | Display the [App Detail page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=xkg1702568563800.html&_LANG=enus "Use a Docusign app's detail page to view important information such as an app summary, features, permissions, and resources.") for detailed information about the app. |
   | Remove App | Remove from the  **List of approved apps**. Also removes the ability to install the app. Note: Existing users can continue to use the app. To restrict all access to an app, uninstall the app. |
   | Uninstall | Uninstall the app from App Center and remove the app for all users. |

title: Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps
heading_path: Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps
text:
You can add apps to an account’s approved apps list from the Select Apps to Approve  section.

1. From the  [Docusign App Center page](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center), select Manage > Approved Apps.

   Note: You can optionally access the Approved Apps page by selecting Extension Apps > Approved Apps.
2. From the Select apps to approve  section, select an app using one of these methods:
   - Select Approve for the app that you want.
   - Search for the app by typing part of the app's name. When the app displays, select Approve.

     ![A screenshot of searching for an app to approve.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-approved-apps-adm-sel-search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MzgsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.NDWEfpPnRIoRvbCuHZte_dphqiwCSJDM8z-toB5OpPY&_LANG=enus)
   - Select one, multiple, or all available apps and choose Add to Approved List.

     ![A screenshot of an App Center approved apps selection example.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-new-sel-apps-to-approve.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MzgsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.NDWEfpPnRIoRvbCuHZte_dphqiwCSJDM8z-toB5OpPY&_LANG=enus)

     The apps move to the List of approved apps.
3. In the overflow menu, select Views Details  to display the [app's Detail page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=xkg1702568563800.html&_LANG=enus "Use a Docusign app's detail page to view important information such as an app summary, features, permissions, and resources.")  for detailed information.

title: Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps
heading_path: Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps
text:
Install an approved app to provide access to account users.

1. From the [Docusign App Center page](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center), select Manage > Approved Apps.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-approve-install-tag.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MzgsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.NDWEfpPnRIoRvbCuHZte_dphqiwCSJDM8z-toB5OpPY&_LANG=enus)
2. Select Install App.

   The Approved  tag changes to Installed and the app is ready for account user access.

title: Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps
heading_path: Administrator: View and Manage Approved Apps
text:
You can remove an app from the approved apps listing.

1. From the [Docusign App Center page](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center), select Manage > Approved Apps.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-approve-install-tag.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MzgsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.NDWEfpPnRIoRvbCuHZte_dphqiwCSJDM8z-toB5OpPY&_LANG=enus)
2. Select the overflow menu and select Remove app.
3. At the confirmation dialog box, select Remove App  to move the app to the Select Apps to approve list.

   ![A screenshot of the Remove app from approved list dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-remove-app-db.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MzgsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.NDWEfpPnRIoRvbCuHZte_dphqiwCSJDM8z-toB5OpPY&_LANG=enus)

   The app is no longer available for account users.

title: Configure a Writeback to HubSpot Step
heading_path: Configure a Writeback to HubSpot Step
text:
Learn to add and configure a Writeback to HubSpot step to update HubSpot records.

Before you begin
:   - Required: Installation of the HubSpot app from
      Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension
      Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more
      information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create
      permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account
      administrator.
    - Required: Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add
      steps like [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus),
      [web forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and
      more to gather recipients, documents, and merge fields for your
      workflow.

You can use a Writeback to HubSpot step to update HubSpot records with
information from your workflow. Administrators link workflow fields to
HubSpot fields in the Writeback to HubSpot step.

When task recipients enter data in workflow fields, Docusign
updates the information in the linked HubSpot record fields.

Writeback improves efficiency and reduces errors caused by manually updating
records.

Example: You can build a workflow that includes a Collect Data
with Web Forms step and a Writeback to HubSpot
step. In the writeback step, you link a HubSpot record's Amount
field to the web form's Amount field.

A workflow recipient updates and signs an agreement for an existing organization named
Fontara. The recipient updates the Fontara agreement's Amount field from $20,000 to
$25,000. When the recipient completes the assignment, Docusign
updates the Fontara record's Amount field to $25,000.

Note: Follow the required formatting for all fields. See the [Supported HubSpot Field Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ref1bcd0a62-7428-42fe-9760-709faa21b8ac.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the HubSpot field types that you can use in Workflow Builder workflows.") topic for more information.

Use the following procedure to create a Writeback to HubSpot
step in a Docusign workflow:

1. Add a Writeback to HubSpot step as a Docusign administrator:
   1. Go to Agreements > Workflows and select Edit next to a workflow
      name.

      The workflow editor page displays.
   2. Select the Add a step
      ![A screenshot displays a plus sign inside of a square.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_add_step_plus_sign.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.PhxSjcOhEGwVrdGAw2ONuwn_4wKGNFGxWFv_7X9KZTw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a plus sign inside of a square.") icon.

      The Add New Step dialog box displays.
   3. Search for steps: Enter `Writeback to
      HubSpot` in the search field and select the
      Writeback to HubSpot step that displays.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Writeback to HubSpot
      step added.
2. Name your Writeback to HubSpot step:
   1. Select Configure on the Writeback to HubSpot
      step.

title: Configure a Writeback to HubSpot Step
heading_path: Configure a Writeback to HubSpot Step
text:
isplays.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Writeback to HubSpot
      step added.
2. Name your Writeback to HubSpot step:
   1. Select Configure on the Writeback to HubSpot
      step.

      The configuration dialog box displays.
   2. Step Name: Keep or edit the name. It is helpful
      to add the name of the HubSpot object, like `Company -
      Writeback to HubSpot`.
3. Where will you write to?
   1. Connection: Choose a HubSpot account that you
      connected to Docusign. Multiple options display if you connected more than one HubSpot
      account.
   2. (Optional) Add New Connection: Select this button if you want to [connect more than one HubSpot
      organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ogk1719256147008.html&_LANG=enus).
   3. (Optional) Manage Connections: Select this
      button if you want to [refresh your HubSpot connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=nta1727383289518&topicId=sqw1734725367259.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. HubSpot object: Search for a HubSpot object or
      choose one from the dropdown menu.
   5. Write settings: Select an option from the list
      that opens:

      - Update or Create: Update a record or
        create a new record if none exists.
      - Update: Update data on an existing
        record.
      - Create: Create a new record.
   6. Select Next.

   ![A screenshot displays a Writeback to HubSpot workflow step with the Connection, HubSpot object, and Write settings highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_HubSpot_Writeback_Where_will_you_write_to_Update_or_create.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.PhxSjcOhEGwVrdGAw2ONuwn_4wKGNFGxWFv_7X9KZTw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Writeback to HubSpot workflow step with the Connection, HubSpot object, and Write settings highlighted.")
4. Which fields are you writing to?: Select workflow fields to
   map to HubSpot fields. When the workflow recipients complete tasks, the data
   they enter updates HubSpot records.

   Note: If you only want to [make an object
   association](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=ref2de68171-0b88-4a3c-a74f-c60b390974fd.html&_LANG=enus), skip to Are you making an association
   with another object?

   1. Select Add or Remove Fields.

      The Add or Remove Fields page displays.
   2. Select the check box next to each HubSpot field that you'd like to use
      as a workflow variable. You'll be able to use these fields in later
      steps.

      ![A screenshot displays the Add or Remove Fields page with HubSpot Owner Email, and HubSpot Owner Name fields selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Writeback_to_HubSpot_Add_or_Remove_Fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.PhxSjcOhEGwVrdGAw2ONuwn_4wKGNFGxWFv_7X9KZTw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Add or Remove Fields page with HubSpot Owner Email, and HubSpot Owner Name fields selected.")
   3. Select Save and Exit.

      The Add or Remove Fields dialog box closes. The fields that you
      selected display.
   4. Fields: Add a variable or step for each
      field:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.

        Note: The name that you enter must exactly
        match an existing field. For example, the rule fails if you
        enter CompanyName for the
        Company\_Name
        variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this workflow:

title: Configure a Writeback to HubSpot Step
heading_path: Configure a Writeback to HubSpot Step
text:
xisting field. For example, the rule fails if you
        enter CompanyName for the
        Company\_Name
        variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this workflow:

        Note: Steps like Read from
        Salesforce and Read from
        HubSpot display fields from up to five
        records. If more than five records are present, HubSpot
        logic decides which five to display. Selecting fields from
        multiple records helps you automate processes like mapping
        multiple HubSpot contacts to multiple Docusign participants.![A screenshot displays read step fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Workflow_field_selections.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.PhxSjcOhEGwVrdGAw2ONuwn_4wKGNFGxWFv_7X9KZTw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays read step fields highlighted.")

        You can use the fields that you select here in later
        steps like Collect Data with
        Webforms or Send Documents
        for Signature.
   5. Repeat this process for each variable displayed.
   6. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays matching Docusign and HubSpot fields with field values highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Writeback_to_HubSpot_Which_fields_are_you_writing_to_Next.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.PhxSjcOhEGwVrdGAw2ONuwn_4wKGNFGxWFv_7X9KZTw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays matching Docusign and HubSpot fields with field values highlighted.")
5. Which record are you writing to?: Create a rule that
   matches each HubSpot field with an equivalent workflow field. This allows
   Workflow Builder to interact with HubSpot data.
   1. HubSpot field: Select a HubSpot field to match
      with a Docusign field.

      Note: When you select a
      (reference) type field, a new field opens
      below it. Choose a child object from the new field.
   2. Operator: Choose an option like Equal
      to, Less than, and so on.
   3. Workflow field: Select a Docusign field to match with your HubSpot field. The following
      options display:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.
      - Select a field from the start step or another workflow
        step.
   4. Select Done to save your rule.

      ![A screenshot displays a HubSpot field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_HubSpot_Writeback_Which_record_are_you_writing_to_rule.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.PhxSjcOhEGwVrdGAw2ONuwn_4wKGNFGxWFv_7X9KZTw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a HubSpot field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.")
6. (Optional) Add more rules:
   - +AND: Workflow Builder only imports HubSpot data that
     matches all of your rules.
   - +OR: Workflow Builder imports HubSpot data that matches
     any of your rules.
7. (Optional) Are you making an association with another object?: Use this procedure if you want to associate objects. If you don’t want to associate objects, select No and Apply:

   Note: Workflow Builder supports [creating primary label associations for HubSpot](https://knowledge.hubspot.com/object-settings/create-and-use-association-labels).
   HubSpot manages the primary label according to their [association rules](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/guides/api/crm/associations/associations-v4).

heading_path: Configure a Writeback to HubSpot Step
text:
ttings/create-and-use-association-labels).
   HubSpot manages the primary label according to their [association rules](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/guides/api/crm/associations/associations-v4).

   1. Select to continue: Select
      Yes.

      Object association fields open.
   2. Object Association (Parent): Choose a HubSpot
      object from the dropdown menu.
   3. Object ID (Parent): Enter an object ID, or
      choose a record ID output from a previous step.

      ![A screenshot displays Object Association fields highlighted connecting HubSpot contacts with a parent record ID.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_HubSpot_Writeback_to_HubSpot_Are_you_making_an_association.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.PhxSjcOhEGwVrdGAw2ONuwn_4wKGNFGxWFv_7X9KZTw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Object Association fields highlighted connecting HubSpot contacts with a parent record ID.")
   4. Select Apply.

   The Writeback to HubSpot dialog box closes. The Workflow Builder page
   displays your new step.

You have completed the process. You have built a Writeback to
HubSpot workflow step. When this step starts, it
updates field data on HubSpot records that you mapped.

title: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
text:
Learn to add and configure a Writeback to Salesforce step to update Salesforce records.

Before you begin
:   - Required: Installation of the Salesforce app
      from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension
      Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) article for more
      information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create
      permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account
      administrator.
    - Required: Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add
      steps like [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus),
      [web forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and
      more to gather recipients, documents, and merge fields for your
      workflow.

You can use a Writeback to Salesforce step to update Salesforce records
with information from your workflow. The Writeback to Salesforce step
updates Salesforce fields, saves documents to records, creates new records, and
more.

Administrators link workflow fields to Salesforce fields in the Writeback to
Salesforce step. When task recipients enter data in workflow fields, Docusign updates the information in the linked Salesforce record
fields.

Writeback improves efficiency and reduces errors caused by manually updating
records.

Example: You can build a workflow that includes a Collect Data
with Web Forms step and a Writeback to
Salesforce step. In the writeback step, you link the Salesforce
opportunity's Amount field to the web form's
Amount field. A workflow recipient updates and signs a
contract for an existing opportunity named Fontara. The recipient updates the
Fontara contract's Amount field from $20,000 to $25,000. When the recipient
completes the assignment, Docusign updates the Fontara record's
Amount field to $25,000.

Note: Follow the required formatting for all fields. See the
[Supported Salesforce Field Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ref769a389d-135f-4fb1-8076-9b01358ffe4d.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the field types that you can use in Salesforce workflow steps.")
topic for more information.

Use the following procedure to create a Writeback to Salesforce
step in a workflow:

1. Add a Writeback to Salesforce step as a Docusign administrator:
   1. Go to Agreements > Workflows and select Edit next to a workflow
      name.

      The workflow editor page displays.
   2. Select the Add a step
      ![A screenshot displays a plus sign inside of a square.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_add_step_plus_sign.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTUsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.TVwZrs3hFgumJZWyHaO0IT_35gmf3hp4DFSftBjpb84&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a plus sign inside of a square.") icon.

      The Add New Step dialog box displays.
   3. Search for steps: Enter `Writeback to
      Salesforce` in the search field and select the
      Writeback to Salesforce step that displays.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Writeback to
      Salesforce step added.
2. Name your Writeback to Salesforce step:
   1. Select Configure on the Writeback to Salesforce
      step.

title: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
text:
The workflow editor page displays with the Writeback to
      Salesforce step added.
2. Name your Writeback to Salesforce step:
   1. Select Configure on the Writeback to Salesforce
      step.

      The configuration dialog box displays.
   2. Step Name: Keep or edit the name. It is helpful
      to add the name of the Salesforce object, like `Account -
      Writeback to Salesforce`.
3. Where will you write to?
   1. Select connection: Select the field and choose a
      Salesforce organization that you connected to Docusign. Multiple options display if you connected more than one Salesforce
      organization.
   2. (Optional) Add New Connection: Select this
      button if you want to [connect more than one Salesforce
      organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ogk1719256147008.html&_LANG=enus).
   3. (Optional) Manage Connections: Select this
      button if you want to [add, edit, refresh, or delete a
      Salesforce connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=uaw1719259450391.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. Salesforce object: Search for an object or
      choose one from the dropdown menu.
   5. Write settings: Select an option from the list
      that opens:

      - Update or Create: Update a record or
        create a new record if none exists.
      - Update: Update data on an existing
        record.
      - Create: Create a new record.
   6. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays a Writeback to Salesforce workflow step with the Connection, Salesforce object, and Write settings highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Writeback_Where_will_you_write_to_Salesforce_object.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTUsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.TVwZrs3hFgumJZWyHaO0IT_35gmf3hp4DFSftBjpb84&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Writeback to Salesforce workflow step with the Connection, Salesforce object, and Write settings highlighted.")
4. Which fields are you writing to?: Select workflow fields to
   map to Salesforce fields. When workflow recipients enter data, Docusign updates Salesforce records.
   1. Select Add or Remove Fields.

      The Add or Remove Fields page displays.
   2. Select the check box next to each Salesforce field that you'd like to
      use as a workflow variable. You'll be able to use these fields in later
      steps.

      ![A screenshot displays the Add or Remove Fields page with two Salesforce fields selected and highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Writeback_to_Salesforce_Add_or_Remove_Fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTUsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.TVwZrs3hFgumJZWyHaO0IT_35gmf3hp4DFSftBjpb84&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Add or Remove Fields page with two Salesforce fields selected and highlighted.")
   3. Select Save and Exit.

      The Add or Remove Fields dialog box closes. The fields that you
      selected display.
   4. Fields: Add a variable or step for each
      field:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.

        Note: The name that you enter must exactly
        match an existing field. For example, the rule fails if you
        enter CompanyName for the
        Company\_Name
        variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

title: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
heading_path: Configure a Writeback to Salesforce Step
text:
field. For example, the rule fails if you
        enter CompanyName for the
        Company\_Name
        variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

        Note: Steps like Read
        from HubSpot and Read from
        Salesforce display fields from up to five
        records. If more than five records are present, Salesforce
        logic decides which five to display. Selecting fields from
        multiple records helps you automate processes like mapping
        multiple Salesforce contacts to multiple Docusign participants.![A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Workflow_field_selections.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTUsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.TVwZrs3hFgumJZWyHaO0IT_35gmf3hp4DFSftBjpb84&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Read from Salesforce fields highlighted.")

        You can use the fields that you select here in
        later steps like Collect Data with
        Webforms or Send Documents for
        Signature.
   5. (Optional) Overwrite if field value exists:
      Select this check box if you want the data entered by a workflow
      participant to overwrite existing Salesforce data.
   6. Repeat this process for each variable displayed.
   7. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays the Account ID Salesforce field and the Overwrite if field value exists checkbox highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_Writeback_to_Salesforce_Which_fields_are_you_writing_to.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTUsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.TVwZrs3hFgumJZWyHaO0IT_35gmf3hp4DFSftBjpb84&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Account ID Salesforce field and the Overwrite if field value exists checkbox highlighted.")
5. Which record are you writing to?: Create a rule that
   matches a Salesforce field with an equivalent workflow field. This allows
   Workflow Builder to interact with Salesforce data.
   1. Salesforce field: Select a Salesforce field to
      match with a Docusign workflow field.

      Note: When you select a
      (reference) type field, a new field opens
      below it. Choose a child object from the new field.
   2. Operator: Choose an option like Equal
      to, Less than, and so on.
   3. Workflow field: Select a Docusign workflow field to match with your Salesforce field. The following
      options display:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.
      - Select a field from the start step or another workflow
        step.
   4. Select Done to save your rule.

      ![A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Writeback_step_Which_record_are_you_writing_to_Rule.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTUsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.TVwZrs3hFgumJZWyHaO0IT_35gmf3hp4DFSftBjpb84&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Salesforce field, an operator value of Greater than or equal to, and a workflow field all highlighted.")
6. (Optional) Add more rules:
   - +AND: Workflow Builder only imports Salesforce data that
     matches all of your rules.
   - +OR: Workflow Builder imports Salesforce data that matches
     any of your rules.
7. Select Apply.

   The Writeback to Salesforce dialog box closes. The page displays your new step.

text:
your rules.
   - +OR: Workflow Builder imports Salesforce data that matches
     any of your rules.
7. Select Apply.

   The Writeback to Salesforce dialog box closes. The page displays your new step.

You have completed the process. You have built a Writeback to
Salesforce step in your workflow. When this step is triggered in a workflow, Docusign updates field data on Salesforce records that you mapped.

title: Delete a Docusign App Connection
text:
Learn how to delete a Docusign app connection. The deleted app
connection is no longer available for Docusign workflows.

You can delete a Docusign app connection in the Docusign
Manage Connections Page.

To learn how to remove Salesforce connections from an agreement workflow, see [Remove a Salesforce Organization From
Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=pwt1719260196069.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Select the Docusign app from the App Center page.
2. On the app detail page, select Manage Connections
   to display the Connections Page.

   ![A screenshot of the Docusign App Connections page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-new-manage-conn-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NjEsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.tvTYPCLOvwLUvy16icIu9Fd2y4Nlt-x9x-mkf5AFSfU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign App Connections page.")
3. Select Delete connection from the overflow menu next to
   display the Delete connection prompt:

   ![A screenshot of the Docusign Delete this Connection dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/prompt-to-delete-connection.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NjEsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.tvTYPCLOvwLUvy16icIu9Fd2y4Nlt-x9x-mkf5AFSfU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign Delete this Connection dialog box.")
4. Enter DELETE.

   Note: Use all uppercase letters to activate the
   Delete Connection option.

The connection is no longer available for Docusign workflows.

Attention: Deleting a connection breaks any platform
service workflow that uses this connection.

title: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance
heading_path: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance
text:
Learn how to set up the ServiceNow instance for use in the Docusign
App Center.

Exchange data between Docusign and
ServiceNow with Docusign IAM for ServiceNow. Use the integration
to kick off software and hardware procurement requests and other downstream
workflows.

To install the app for use with Docusign Workflow Builder,
follow these steps. If you run into any issues, see [Troubleshoot Installing the Docusign IAM for ServiceNow App](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=bur1727290839878.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to troubleshoot issues with installing Docusign IAM for ServiceNow.").

1. [Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 1: Install the Docusign IAM for ServiceNow App](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=dnf1727290653940.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install the Docusign IAM app in ServiceNow.").
2. [Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 2: Generate a New Client Secret for OAuth](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=jgz1727290675860.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a new client secret for Docusign IAM for ServiceNow.").
3. [Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 3: Update the System Property](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=bid1727290690554.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to update the system property in ServiceNow.").
4. [Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 4: Assign the User Role](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=qvz1727290705170.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to assign the user role for the Docusign IAM for ServiceNow app.").
5. [Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 5: Assign Application Access to ServiceNow Tables](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=sdo1727290722252.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to grant Docusign access to ServiceNow tables.").
6. [Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 6: Create Access Controls](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=bvm1727290749720.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create access controls so that Docusign can access ServiceNow tables.").
7. [Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 7: Create Cross-Scope Privilege Records](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=yjb1727290763658.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create cross-scope privilege records so that Docusign can access ServiceNow tables.").
8. [Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 8: Install the ServiceNow App From the Docusign App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=mmt1727290788022.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install the ServiceNow app for use with Docusign Workflow Builder.").

You completed this task. Your ServiceNow instance is ready
for use with Docusign Workflow Builder.

title: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance
heading_path: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance
text: Move to [Configure a ServiceNow Writeback Step in an Agreement Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=zqo1712846235553.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about using the ServiceNow app to run multiple business applications per instance through Docusign agreement workflows.").

title: Reconnect a Docusign App Connection
text:
Learn how to use the Docusign app Reconnect a connection feature to reestablish the Docusign app’s connection to workflows.

If there is a connection disconnect, select Docusign app Reconnect a connection to reestablish the app’s connection to workflows.

For information about reconnecting Salesforce connections, see [Reconnect a Salesforce Organization to Your Workflow Builder App](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=kam1719258759323.html&_LANG=enus)

1. Select the Docusign app.
2. On the app detail page, select Manage Connections to access the
   Connections page.

   ![A screenshot of a sample App Center Connections page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-conn-page-ex.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NjMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.Pomgb87gnPQ_8pIT3VFHlQUm5QSJP7QSXZvSeGNEwg4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample App Center Connections page.")
3. For the connection you want to reconnect, select Reconnect from the overflow menu.
4. At the Reconnect this connection dialog box, type RECONNECT and select Reconnect.

The Docusign for Salesforce app connection to
workflows is available or re-established.

title: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 4: Assign the User Role
heading_path: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 4: Assign the User Role
text:
Learn how to assign the user role for the Docusign IAM for ServiceNow
app.

Exchange data between Docusign and ServiceNow with Docusign IAM for ServiceNow. Use the integration to kick off software and hardware
procurement requests and other downstream workflows.

This topic shows the part of the authorization process where you assign the user
role.

1. Select the All tab in the top menu. Search for and
   select Users.

   The Users page displays a list of
   users.
2. At the top right, select New to create a new user.

   The User New Record page displays.
3. Enter the details for the new user and select
   Submit.

   You return to the list of users.
4. Search for and open the user record that you just created and want to grant access to.
5. In the Roles tab, select
   Edit.

   ![A screenshot of the page for the user record. The Roles tab is highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/servicenowrolestab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MzcsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.-at-Fd6hTBYvaiyT4kqgPh3q57Xfhq0VBqxb2YA37mw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the page for the user record. The Roles tab is highlighted.")

   The Edit Members page displays. A list of available
   roles displays on the left. The user and any current roles assigned to them
   display on the right.
6. In the Collection search box on the left, search for the
   role
   x\_dosi\_docusign\_in.docusign\_scripted\_rest\_api\_access\_role.
   Select it in the list on the left and select the right arrow.

   ![A screenshot of the Edit Members page showing the available roles on the left and assigned roles on the right.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/servicenowassignrole.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MzcsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.-at-Fd6hTBYvaiyT4kqgPh3q57Xfhq0VBqxb2YA37mw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Edit Members page showing the available roles on the left and assigned roles on the right.")

   This role authorizes a user's admin**read from**and **writeback**access to the IAM
   Spoke app APIs.
7. When you are finished assigning roles to the user, select
   Save.

You completed this task. The required role is
assigned.

title: Edit Docusign App Connections
text:
Learn how to update Docusign app connection names in the Docusign App Center Manage Connections page.

You can change your Docusign app connection name to something more
specific or meaningful.

Editing a name does not change the name of the account. It only changes how it displays
in workflows.

To learn how to edit Salesforce connections for an agreement workflow, see [Edit the Name of a Salesforce Organization
Connected to Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=naa1719257879043.html&_LANG=enus).

Note:

Currently, you can only rename app connections. You cannot change:

- The connection type. That is, toggle from Shared to
  Private.
- Connection ownership.

To make these changes, create a new app connection with the characteristics you want
as described in the [Create Docusign App Connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=zsb1714159452358.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create Docusign apps connections for different user credentials in Docusign agreement workflows. You can create connections that are shared by all account users or used privately by the user who created the app connection.") article.

1. Select the Docusign app from the App Center page.
2. On the app detail page, select Manage Connections to access the
   Connections page.

   ![A screenshot of a sample App Center Connection page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-conn-dash-inst-apps.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NTYsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.YfjqCLJWpQI1C22VkHuZT3vSJGk46Jci5hcuVbnDppk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample App Center Connection page.")
3. From the Overflow menu, select Edit connection name.

   The Edit connection name dialog box displays.

   ![A screenshot of the Docusign Edit connection name dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-edit-conname-db.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NTYsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.YfjqCLJWpQI1C22VkHuZT3vSJGk46Jci5hcuVbnDppk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign Edit connection name dialog box.")
4. Enter a new name and select Save.

   The system displays a confirmation message:

   `Successfully edited connection`

title: Configure a Dropbox Store Files Step in an Agreement Workflow
heading_path: Configure a Dropbox Store Files Step in an Agreement Workflow
text:
Learn how to configure a Dropbox
store
files step in a Docusign agreement workflow.

Use the Dropbox
store
files step to save documents generated by workflow steps.
Generally, you position your archive step at the end of your workflow to archive
completed documents.

Restriction: Do not use a Dropbox
store
files step if your workflow uses an eSignature step with multiple
participants and your first signer is designated as an embedded signer.
In this scenario, the first signer goes directly to sign from a prior workflow step.
Documents are archived directly after the embedded signer finishes signing and would
not include the actions of any subsequent participants.

Before you begin
:   - You must be an account administrator or have Workflows: Create user permissions as described in the [Workflow Builder Roles and Permission Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) article.
    - The extension app must be [installed and authorized](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus) on your account.

1. Create a new workflow as described in the [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus) article.
2. Select Add a step >  Store files with Dropbox >  Configure.
3. Select the file to archive and select Apply.
4. Specify where to archive the file:
   1. Select the connection.
   2. Select the site.
   3. Select a drive.
   4. (Optional) Select a folder on the drive by selecting Add
      Folder. All existing folders are listed, as well as an
      option to create a new folder.
5. Specify a name for your archive. Use the text and variable controls to build
   your file name.

   ![A screenshot of the Dropbox Archive naming dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/acarchivewhat.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4ODUsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.5upAkB5FUClk4CztbO0RYu6eQLQ4iA5NRgwGunxxUxM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Dropbox Archive naming dialog box.")
6. Review the completed file path and select Apply.

Your Dropbox
store
files step is configured.

title: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 5: Assign Application Access to ServiceNow Tables
heading_path: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 5: Assign Application Access to ServiceNow Tables
text:
Learn how to grant Docusign access to ServiceNow tables.

Exchange data between Docusign and ServiceNow with Docusign IAM for ServiceNow. Use the integration to kick off software and hardware
procurement requests and other downstream workflows.

This topic shows how to grant Docusign access to ServiceNow tables.

1. At the top right, select the globe icon. Make sure that
   the application scope is the same as the scope of the table that you are
   granting access to.
2. Select the All tab from the top menu. Search for and
   select tables.

   The Tables page displays.
3. Select a table that you want Docusign to access.

   The details page for the table displays.
4. In the Application Access tab, for the
   Accessible from field, select All
   application scopes.
5. Click the Edit Herebutton. Check the boxes for
   Can read, Can create, and
   Can update and select
   Update.

   ![A screenshot of the details page for the table showing all the required information completed.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/servicenowassignscopes.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MzksInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.VHNdd78pn0WeiU-NjkFlRJR4dfraUCKC1yNzmwU0F9s&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the details page for the table showing all the required information completed.")

You completed this task. You granted Docusign
access to a ServiceNow table.

title: Docusign App Center Regional Availability
text:
Learn about the regional availability for Docusign App Center apps and
where to find this information in Docusign App Center.

Docusign App Center app availability can vary by region. Each app's [App Detail Page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=xkg1702568563800.html&_LANG=enus "Use a Docusign app's detail page to view important information such as an app summary, features, permissions, and resources.") includes the supported region.

Apps are available in these geographical areas and include one of these designations.

| Available Region | App Detail Page Designation | Definition |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Globally available in all regions | Global | Worldwide |
| Only the NA region | North America | United States and Latin America |
| Only the EU the site | EMEA | All EU countries and the UK |
| Only the Japan site | Japan | Japan |
| Only the Canada site | Canada | Canada |
| Only the Australia site | Australia | Australia |
| Available in NA, EU, UK, CA, and AU | North America  EMEA  Canada  AU | United States, Latin America, EU, UK, Canada, and Australia |

title: Configure a Stripe Writeback Step in an Agreement Workflow
heading_path: Configure a Stripe Writeback Step in an Agreement Workflow
text:
Learn how to use the Stripe extension app to streamline automation for payments,
billing, and subscriptions through Docusign agreement
workflows.

title: Configure a Stripe Writeback Step in an Agreement Workflow
heading_path: Configure a Stripe Writeback Step in an Agreement Workflow
text:
- Be an account administrator or have Workflows: Create user permissions as described in the
  [Workflow Builder Roles and Permission Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html)
  article.
- The Stripe extension app must be [installed and authorized](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus) on your account.
- Complete these steps:
  - [Set Up Docusign Workflow Builder for Stripe](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qzg1726786604706&topicId=ubn1727888317315.html&_LANG=enus)
  - [Install Docusign for Stripe](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qzg1726786604706&topicId=ols1724269694863.html&_LANG=enus)
- To create a step that writes an invoice or invoice item object to Stripe, first set up a product
  in Stripe.

The Stripe extension app lets you streamline automation for payments, billing, and
subscriptions through Docusign Workflow Builder agreement workflows.

1. Create a new workflow as described in the [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus) article.
2. Select Add a step  >  Writeback to Stripe  >  Configure.

   The step is added to the workflow.
3. (Optional) The default Step Name is Writeback
   to Stripe. Change this to a more meaningful name, such as
   Create Stripe Customer.
4. Where will you write to?
   1. Connection: Make sure that the
      Connection type is
      StripeOAuth.
   2. Stripe object: Select an object from the
      dropdown menu.
   3. Write settings: Select an option from the list
      that opens:

      - Update or Create: Update a record or
        create a new record if none exists.
      - Update: Update data on an existing
        record.
      - Create: Create a new record.

      ![A screenshot displays the Stripe Write settings options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/write-to-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTksInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.DbuKLJbgFO5gfumUjhJD4tXZr-yCGym432Dntg4yAWM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Stripe Write settings options.")
   4. Select Next.
5. Where
   will you
   writeto?:
   Select
   a Stripe object. When the workflow recipients complete tasks,
   the data they enter updates field data on Stripe records.

   ![A screenshot displays sample Stripe Write fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/where-will-you-write-to.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTksInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.DbuKLJbgFO5gfumUjhJD4tXZr-yCGym432Dntg4yAWM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays sample Stripe Write fields.")

   1. Select Add or Remove Fields.

      The Add or Remove Fields page displays.
   2. Select Stripe fields that you'd like to use as workflow variables.
      You'll be able to use them in later steps.

      ![A screenshot displays field values for the Company object.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/add-or-remove-fields-for-stripe-writeback.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTksInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.DbuKLJbgFO5gfumUjhJD4tXZr-yCGym432Dntg4yAWM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays field values for the Company object.")
   3. Select Save and Exit.

      The Add or Remove Fields dialog box closes. The fields you
      selected display.
   4. Select the Select field below a Stripe field
      name.
   5. Choose an existing workflow field or create a new field:

      - Enter manually: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.

title: Configure a Stripe Writeback Step in an Agreement Workflow
heading_path: Configure a Stripe Writeback Step in an Agreement Workflow
text:
Select the Select field below a Stripe field
      name.
   5. Choose an existing workflow field or create a new field:

      - Enter manually: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.

        Note: The name you enter must exactly match
        an existing field. For example, the rule fails if you enter
        CompanyName and the variable you
        want is Company\_Name.
      - Starting Variables: Find custom variables
        created outside of the workflow steps.
      - WORKFLOW STEPS: Find variables from other
        workflow steps.
      - OTHER SOURCES: Choose
        Variables from Workflow Start,
        Instance details, or other
        types.
   6. Repeat this process for each variable displayed.

      ![A screenshot displays matching Workflow Builder and Stripe fields with field values highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/acstripewhichfieldsdb.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTksInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.DbuKLJbgFO5gfumUjhJD4tXZr-yCGym432Dntg4yAWM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays matching Workflow Builder and Stripe fields with field values highlighted.")
   7. Select Next.
6. Which record are you writing to?: Create a rule that
   matches each Stripe field with the equivalent workflow field. This allows
   Workflow Builder to read Stripe data. You can create as many rules as you want:
   1. Stripe field: Select a Stripe field to match
      with a Docusign workflow field.
   2. Operator: Keep Equal to.
   3. Workflow field: Select a Docusign workflow field to match with your Stripe
      field. The following options display:

      - Enter manually: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.

        Note: The name you enter must exactly match
        an existing field. For example, the rule fails if you enter
        CompanyName and the variable you
        want is Company\_Name.
      - Starting Variables: Find custom variables
        created outside of the workflow steps.
      - WORKFLOW STEPS: Find variables from other
        workflow steps.
      - OTHER SOURCES: Choose
        Variables from Workflow Start,
        Instance details, or other
        types.
   4. Select Done to save your rule.

      ![A screenshot displays the Stripe Company Name field related to the Contact ID field, with the Done button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/acstripewritetorecord.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MTksInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.DbuKLJbgFO5gfumUjhJD4tXZr-yCGym432Dntg4yAWM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Stripe Company Name field related to the Contact ID field, with the Done button highlighted.")
   5. (Optional) Add more rules:

      - +AND: Add a new rule. Workflow Builder only writes
        back to Stripe fields that match all of your rules.
      - +OR: Add a new rule. Workflow Builder writes back
        to Stripe fields that match any of your rules.
7. Select Apply.

   You have completed the process. You have built a Writeback to Stripe
   step in your Workflow Builder workflow. When this step is generated in a workflow, it
   updates field data on Stripe records you mapped.

You completed this task. You created and configured a
Writeback to Stripe step. You return to the workflow overview in the Workflow Builder
workflow.

title: Access Docusign eSignature Integrations in App Center
heading_path: Access Docusign eSignature Integrations in App Center
text:
Learn about the Docusign eSignature integration apps available in
Docusign App Center.

With Docusign eSignature integration apps, you can easily add Docusign functionality to your favorite software tools without
leaving them.

For information about eSignature integrations see [Streamline process with 1,000+ integrations](https://www.docusign.com/integrations?filter=Integrations).

These eSignature integration apps are currently available:

- eSignature for Google Workspace
- eSignature for HubSpot
- eSignature for Microsoft Dynamics
- eSignature for Microsoft PowerAutomate
- eSignature for Microsoft SharePoint
- eSignature for Microsoft Teams
- eSignature for Salesforce
- eSignature for ServiceNow
- eSignature for Workday
- ID Verification with Clear

As an Admin, when you access an app's detail page, install the application
so that it's available for your users.

1. Access [Docusign App Center](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center).
2. From the left navigation pane, select eSignature
   integrations to display the available eSignature apps.

   ![A screenshot of the Docusign App Center eSignature integrations page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-esig-int-dash.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE3ODQsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.CGHQ2nkXoX9VRZhKj_LUhHcHpx0MYhCV6k9CzZLu_uU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign App Center eSignature integrations page.")
3. Select an application to display the detail page.
4. Select Install.

   - If the app has not been installed, you're directed to an external
     site. Here you can access the app as shown in this example.

     ![A screenshot of a sample App Center eSignature integration app that needs installation.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-esig-int-not-inst.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE3ODQsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.CGHQ2nkXoX9VRZhKj_LUhHcHpx0MYhCV6k9CzZLu_uU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample App Center eSignature integration app that needs installation.")

     Obtain and install the app in App Center as described in the [Install Apps in the Docusign App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install Docusign extension apps in the Docusign App Center.") article.
   - If your administrator installed the app, a message similar to this
     example displays.

     ![A screenshot of a sample App Center eSignature integration app that has been installed.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-esig-int-already-inst.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE3ODQsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.CGHQ2nkXoX9VRZhKj_LUhHcHpx0MYhCV6k9CzZLu_uU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample App Center eSignature integration app that has been installed.")

     Use the app as described in the [Non-admin Users: Work with Approved
     Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=xni1748970087448.html&_LANG=enus "As a Docusign account user, learn how to find and manage Docusign apps you’re approved to use.")  article.

title: Access Docusign eSignature Integrations in App Center
heading_path: Access Docusign eSignature Integrations in App Center
text:
Learn about the Docusign eSignature integration apps available in
Docusign App Center.

With Docusign eSignature integration apps, you can easily add Docusign functionality to your favorite software tools without
leaving them.

For information about eSignature integrations see [Streamline process with 1,000+ integrations](https://www.docusign.com/integrations?filter=Integrations).

These eSignature integration apps are currently available:

- eSignature for Google Workspace
- eSignature for HubSpot
- eSignature for Microsoft Dynamics
- eSignature for Microsoft PowerAutomate
- eSignature for Microsoft SharePoint
- eSignature for Microsoft Teams
- eSignature for Salesforce
- eSignature for ServiceNow
- eSignature for Workday
- ID Verification with Clear

As an Admin, when you access an app's detail page, install the application
so that it's available for your users.

1. Access [Docusign App Center](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center).
2. From the left navigation pane, select eSignature
   integrations to display the available eSignature apps.
