   ![A screenshot of the Docusign App Center eSignature integrations page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-esig-int-dash.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MDMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.rDhJuQaAUIl-qhKqStAL8jhvlQvEd0vxsEDgcQCjUmY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign App Center eSignature integrations page.")
3. Select an application to display the detail page.
4. Select Install.

   - If the app has not been installed, you're directed to an external
     site. Here you can access the app as shown in this example.

     ![A screenshot of a sample App Center eSignature integration app that needs installation.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-esig-int-not-inst.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MDMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.rDhJuQaAUIl-qhKqStAL8jhvlQvEd0vxsEDgcQCjUmY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample App Center eSignature integration app that needs installation.")

     Obtain and install the app in App Center as described in the [Install Apps in the Docusign App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install Docusign extension apps in the Docusign App Center.") article.
   - If your administrator installed the app, a message similar to this
     example displays.

     ![A screenshot of a sample App Center eSignature integration app that has been installed.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-esig-int-already-inst.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MDMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.rDhJuQaAUIl-qhKqStAL8jhvlQvEd0vxsEDgcQCjUmY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample App Center eSignature integration app that has been installed.")

     Use the app as described in the [Non-admin Users: Work with Approved
     Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=xni1748970087448.html&_LANG=enus "As a Docusign account user, learn how to find and manage Docusign apps you’re approved to use.")  article.

title: Access Docusign App Center Connections
text:
Learn how to add a new Docusign app connection for a feature workflow
from the Docusign App Center.

You can add a Docusign app connection to a feature workflow from the
App Center main page.

To learn how to add Salesforce connections to an agreement workflow, see the [Connect Workflow Builder to Multiple Salesforce
Organizations](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ogk1719256147008.html&_LANG=enus) article.

1. Select Manage Apps from the App Center main menu.
2. Select Installed Apps from the side menu.

   ![A screenshot of a sample Docusign Manage Connections page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-conn-dash-inst-apps.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NTEsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.Sp9IxaDDooETgbuzTFV9KgwZ3wOKwZWbZigMb0fDdYs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample Docusign Manage Connections page.")

   Note: You can optionally access the app you want by
   selecting Extension Apps in the App Center side
   menu.
3. Select an app to display the app's detail page.

   ![A screenshot showing the menu to manage connections from the App Detail page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-conn-menu-det-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NTEsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.Sp9IxaDDooETgbuzTFV9KgwZ3wOKwZWbZigMb0fDdYs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing the menu to manage connections from the App Detail page.")

   Note: Only Admins can uninstall
   apps.
4. Depending on the selected app, if there are connections for the app, select
   Manage and choose a connection management action from
   the overflow menu.
5. If there are no connections, select Add New
   Connection.

   Follow the instructions in the [Create App
   Connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=zsb1714159452358.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create Docusign apps connections for different user credentials in Docusign agreement workflows. You can create connections that are shared by all account users or used privately by the user who created the app connection.") article to add your connections.

title: Remove or Disable a Docusign App with an Associated Rule
heading_path: Remove or Disable a Docusign App with an Associated Rule
text:
Learn how to make adjustments in Docusign Account Administration when
you use Docusign App Center to disable or remove an app that has
associated rules.

Through Docusign Account Administration, you created and manage agreement action rules to automate feature actions as described in the [Create an Agreement Rule](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=wqg1596767500566.html&_LANG=enus)﻿﻿ article.

In App Center, if you disable or remove an app that has associated rules, the system
displays a important message as shown in this example.

![A screenshot showing a sample message when you attempt to delete or disable an app connection with an associated rule.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-app-con-disable-app-msg.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MjQsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.1pA0OLHltlFAG_z8IpPTglok9B70tR9E3QsyDiA3Dus&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing a sample message when you attempt to delete or disable an app connection with an associated rule.")

Make adjustments in Docusign Account Administration to prevent
problems with applications.

1. Select an app in the App Center.
2. From the overflow menu, select Disable or
   Remove to modify the app.

   ![A screenshot showing the App Center Disable or Remove menu choices.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-app-con-disable-app.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MjQsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.1pA0OLHltlFAG_z8IpPTglok9B70tR9E3QsyDiA3Dus&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing the App Center Disable or Remove menu choices.")
3. If you receive the message shown in this example, go to the RULES listing page
   in Account Administration as described in the [Agreement Rules Modification](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=cpx1599852519793.html&_LANG=enus) article.

   ![A screenshot showing a sample message when you attempt to delete or disable an app connection with an associated rule.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-app-con-disable-app-msg.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MjQsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.1pA0OLHltlFAG_z8IpPTglok9B70tR9E3QsyDiA3Dus&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing a sample message when you attempt to delete or disable an app connection with an associated rule.")
4. For the rule in question, hover over the alert icon to display the message as
   shown in this example.

   ![A screenshot showing an example of the RULES page with a rule alert.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-app-con-disable-agree-rules.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MjQsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.1pA0OLHltlFAG_z8IpPTglok9B70tR9E3QsyDiA3Dus&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing an example of the RULES page with a rule alert.")
5. Select the Action Menu and select
   Edit.

   ![A screenshot showing a sample Agreement RULES page ACTION menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-app-con-agree-rule-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MjQsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.1pA0OLHltlFAG_z8IpPTglok9B70tR9E3QsyDiA3Dus&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing a sample Agreement RULES page ACTION menu.")
6. On the Edit Rules page, a message displays as shown in this example.

title: Remove or Disable a Docusign App with an Associated Rule
heading_path: Remove or Disable a Docusign App with an Associated Rule
text:
c0OCJ9.1pA0OLHltlFAG_z8IpPTglok9B70tR9E3QsyDiA3Dus&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing a sample Agreement RULES page ACTION menu.")
6. On the Edit Rules page, a message displays as shown in this example.

   ![A screenshot of a sample Agreement rules inactive rule warning message.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-app-con-agree-rule-edit-pg.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MjQsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.1pA0OLHltlFAG_z8IpPTglok9B70tR9E3QsyDiA3Dus&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample Agreement rules inactive rule warning message.")
7. Change the rule's fields and save the rule to eliminate the warning
   message.

title: Configure a Store Files in Dynamics 365 Step in an Agreement Workflow
heading_path: Configure a Store Files in Dynamics 365 Step in an Agreement Workflow
text: Learn how to configure a Store Files in Dynamics 365 step in an Agreement Workflow.

title: Configure a Store Files in Dynamics 365 Step in an Agreement Workflow
heading_path: Configure a Store Files in Dynamics 365 Step in an Agreement Workflow
text:
- Required: Installation of the **Dynamics 365** app from Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.
- Required: [Workflows Roles and Permission Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account administrator.
- Required: Access to a suitable template to use for your workflow step. Review the [Template Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=xnw1709852160602.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.
- Recommended: Open the template in another window to view as you set up your step. This helps you to correctly configure your Store Files in Dynamics 365 step.

The **Store Files in Dynamics 365** step adds signed agreements and certificates of completion to
Dynamics 365 entities. Administrators choose files to store, a location to store them, and a naming
convention for the files. The **Store Files in Dynamics 365** step decides the name and storage location of agreements sent from workflows.

**For Example**: You build a workflow with a **Store Files in Dynamics 365** step. Your workflow
sends an agreement to three recipients. When all three recipients sign the agreement, Docusign
uses **Store Files in Dynamics 365** variables to add the agreement to the correct record.

**Procedure**

1. Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add steps like [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus), [web forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and more to gather recipients, documents, and merge fields for your workflow.
2. (Optional): Add the Read from Dynamics 365 step to your workflow. See the [Configure a Read From Dynamics 365 Step in Agreement Workflows﻿﻿](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pic1741250291019&topicId=irr1741677562959.html) article for details.
3. Select Add a step > Store Files in Dynamics 365 > Configure.
4. Step Name: Keep or edit the name.
5. What are you storing?: Select a file or envelope from an earlier step in the workflow and
   select Next.
6. Where are you storing it?:
   1. Select connection: Choose a Dynamics 365 organization you connected to
      Workflow Builder.
   2. Select record: Select the Microsoft Dynamics 365 record ID (for example,
      Opportunity).
   3. Microsoft Dynamics 365 object: Select the appropriate Microsoft Dynamics 365 object from the dropdown list.

      Note: If you add the Read from Dynamics 365 step and select a record ID from the same step, the Dynamics 365 object is automatically populated. You can edit the Dynamics 365 object, if required.
   4. Select Next.

title: Configure a Store Files in Dynamics 365 Step in an Agreement Workflow
heading_path: Configure a Store Files in Dynamics 365 Step in an Agreement Workflow
text:
add the Read from Dynamics 365 step and select a record ID from the same step, the Dynamics 365 object is automatically populated. You can edit the Dynamics 365 object, if required.
   4. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot of the selected connection, record, and Dynamics 365 object for the workflow.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/iam-sales-dyn365-3.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4ODgsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.ndu8AXtFDJS5UuEyqdJV9GJ1X5JtUE6ikqggUGc-ens&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the selected connection, record, and Dynamics 365 object for the workflow.")
7. What will you call it?: Use variables and text to create a custom file name for your
   agreements:
   1. Add Variable: Use the search field or dropdown menu to select variables to add to
      the agreement name.
   2. (Optional) Add text: Enter text to add to the agreement name and select Add.

      Note: The File path field displays the naming convention. As you add variables and
      text, the name updates. **Example**: File path Microsoft Dynamics 365 1
      Shared/[Envelope ID]FontaraNDA.

      ![A screenshot of the variables and text used to create a custom file name for your agreements.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ds-dyn365-app-store_file2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4ODgsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.ndu8AXtFDJS5UuEyqdJV9GJ1X5JtUE6ikqggUGc-ens&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the variables and text used to create a custom file name for your agreements.")
   3. Select Apply.

      The Store Files in Dynamics 365 column closes. The workflow page
      displays your new step.

You have completed the process. You have added a **Store Files in Dynamics 365** step to your
workflow. When signers complete an agreement, Docusign adds the agreement and
certificate of completion to the specified record.

title: Access Docusign Workflow Builder Templates in the App Center
heading_path: Access Docusign Workflow Builder Templates in the App Center
text:
Learn how to select and initiate a Docusign Workflow Builder template from the Docusign App Center page.

You can select and initiate a Docusign Workflow Builder template from the Docusign App Center page.

1. Access workflow templates using one of these methods to display available templates:

   - Featured
     Workflow templates section: Select an available workflow
     template from the list or by selecting See All Templates.

     ![A screenshot showing an sample Workflow Builder template from the Featured Workflow section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-new-workflow-temp-ex.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MTAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.LO2-X7vuoFeQ_7qr2kFCPfkscxcMOr_fnyrzjIu64gc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing an sample Workflow Builder template from the Featured Workflow section.")
   - An App Detail page: In the Related workflow templates  section, select a template to display the Workflow template
     page. Review the information to learn about the workflow template.

     ![A screenshot of a sample Docusign workflow detail page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-workflow-det-pg.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MTAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.LO2-X7vuoFeQ_7qr2kFCPfkscxcMOr_fnyrzjIu64gc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample Docusign workflow detail page.")

     Note:
     - If the associated app is installed, create a workflow
       template by selecting Use
       Template.
     - If the associated app is not installed, log on and install
       the app. After installation, you can use the associated
       workflow template.
2. Optionally, you can narrow your search by selecting filters:

   - Industry menu: Select an Industry sector
   - Steps: Select a workflow step.

   This example shows the resulting workflow template based on the selected
   Works with and Industry
   filters.

   ![A screenshot of a workflow template selection using Industry and step type.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-new-wf-filter-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MTAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.LO2-X7vuoFeQ_7qr2kFCPfkscxcMOr_fnyrzjIu64gc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a workflow template selection using Industry and step type.")
3. Select a workflow template and select Use.

   The Workflow Builder workflow template displays in a new window.

   ![A screenshot of a sample Docusign Workflow Builder Workflow that is ready to use.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-workflow-access-results.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MTAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.LO2-X7vuoFeQ_7qr2kFCPfkscxcMOr_fnyrzjIu64gc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample Docusign Workflow Builder Workflow that is ready to use.")

   See the [Workflow Builder Workflow Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=zbf1698707257742.html&_LANG=enus) article for
   information about using Workflow Builder Workflows.

title: Access Docusign Workflow Builder Templates in the App Center
heading_path: Access Docusign Workflow Builder Templates in the App Center
text:
You can view information about a Workflow Builder workflow to
learn about its features and how to use it in your workflow.

1. In the App Center's Featured Workflow templates
   section, select a workflow template.

   This example shows a workflow detail page.
2. Depending on the app, log on to use it.
3. Optionally view additional information.

   - Instructional video
   - Template overview
   - Template requirements
   - Setup guidelines

   This example shows a sample workflow template details page.

   ![A screenshot of a sample workflow details page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-workflow-det-pg.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MTAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.LO2-X7vuoFeQ_7qr2kFCPfkscxcMOr_fnyrzjIu64gc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample workflow details page.")

title: Use the Learn Feature to Access App Learning Resources
heading_path: Use the Learn Feature to Access App Learning Resources
text:
Use the Docusign App Center Learn tab to access a centralized learning resource for apps and integration.

Use the App Center Learn tab to access a centralized learning resource for apps and integration.

This feature is currently under development, providing quick start information for Salesforce. It
will provide links to Docusign Support Center articles,
partner documentation, videos, and other resources.

- The App Center dashboard Learn  tab
- An individual app's detail page

You can access the Learn information from:

1. Access [Docusign App Center](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center).
2. From the top navigation menu, select the Learn
   tab.
3. On the Learn page, browse the tiles and select the content
   you want to open.

   If the guide includes platform tabs (such as Salesforce or HubSpot), select
   the tab that matches your platform.

title: Access AI Integrations in App Center
heading_path: Access AI Integrations in App Center
text:
Learn about the AI connector apps available in Docusign App Center.

Docusign AI connectors are MCP-based integrations that expose Docusign IAM products inside third-party AI and business
platforms. These platforms can include Claude, Copilot, Agentforce, ChatGPT, Slack,
and Teams. Users can securely access agreement data and actions directly from those
tools.

You can access these AI connector apps in Docusign App
Center:

- Anthropic Claude: The Docusign Connector for Anthropic Claude provides Claude secure agreement intelligence and eSignature capabilities. See the [Docusign Connector in Anthropic Claude﻿ article](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ug3906200f-95c6-4a6b-90b1-f928c85961c6&topicId=con54692874-8229-4739-916f-fdb70778038a.html&_LANG=enus) for more information.
- Google Gemini: The Docusign connector in Gemini Enterprise acts as a secure bridge between your Google Workspace environment and your agreement data. See the  [Docusign Connector in Gemini Enterprise](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ug3906200f-95c6-4a6b-90b1-f928c85961c6&topicId=con7d418007-d283-41eb-8ea6-6d28e80c161d.html&_LANG=enus) article for more information.
- ChatGPT: The Docusign connector in ChatGPT provides an advanced AI chatbot and virtual assistant. See the [Bringing Our Leading Contract AI to ChatGPT](https://www.docusign.com/blog/docusign-contract-ai-chatgpt) article for more information.

Requirements

- Anthropic Claude: Requires an Anthropic Claude Pro, Team, or Enterprise account.
- Google Gemini: Requires a Google Gemini Enterprise, Premier, or Business plan.
- ChatGPT: Requires ChatGPT Pro, Team, or Enterprise plan.

1. Access  [Docusign App Center](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center).
2. From the left navigation pane, select IA Connectors and
   select the AI connector that you want.

   ![A screenshot of the App Center AI Connectors apps.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-ai-con-pg.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE3ODEsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.vgoaz-2vs5SwxW49pkpPS2MMufaJSOJfYI4wYOEEXrk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the App Center AI Connectors apps.")
3. Select an AI connector to display the app's Detail page.

   ![A screenshot of the App Center Claude Cowork detail page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-claude-ai-det-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE3ODEsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.vgoaz-2vs5SwxW49pkpPS2MMufaJSOJfYI4wYOEEXrk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the App Center Claude Cowork detail page.")
4. On the app detail page, select Join Waitlist.

   You are redirected to the Docusign MCP Beta Program: Request Access external site to access the MCP Server. See the [Build with Docusign MCP Server (Beta)](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/mcp-server/?utm_source=google&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_NEW_2401_SEMBranded&utm_term=docusign%20mcp&campaignid=23603370651&adgroupid=194748469858&location=9198112&device=c&matchtype=p&extensionid=&creative=798619703005&keyword=docusign%20mcp&placement=&network=g&gclid=CjwKCAjw5ZXQBhBdEiwAI5XVWQTzBj5923Le_p3zGKAXzdbxEgXEIgqEwEd7hGkzMZnIrl0oCZzIfRoC_AwQAvD_BwE&gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=23603370651&gbraid=0AAAAADicDKv84OcGWQ2OMFJBG1oPYGzJh&gclid=CjwKCAjw5ZXQBhBdEiwAI5XVWQTzBj5923Le_p3zGKAXzdbxEgXEIgqEwEd7hGkzMZnIrl0oCZzIfRoC_AwQAvD_BwE) article for information about this program.
5. Select Start  to sign up access for the Beta program to use the AI connector.

title: Access AI Integrations in App Center
heading_path: Access AI Integrations in App Center
text:
Learn about the AI connector apps available in Docusign App Center.

Docusign AI connectors are MCP-based integrations that expose Docusign IAM products inside third-party AI and business
platforms. These platforms can include Claude, Copilot, Agentforce, ChatGPT, Slack,
and Teams. Users can securely access agreement data and actions directly from those
tools.

You can access these AI connector apps in Docusign App
Center:

- Anthropic Claude: The Docusign Connector for Anthropic Claude provides Claude secure agreement intelligence and eSignature capabilities. See the [Docusign Connector in Anthropic Claude﻿ article](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ug3906200f-95c6-4a6b-90b1-f928c85961c6&topicId=con54692874-8229-4739-916f-fdb70778038a.html&_LANG=enus) for more information.
- Google Gemini: The Docusign connector in Gemini Enterprise acts as a secure bridge between your Google Workspace environment and your agreement data. See the  [Docusign Connector in Gemini Enterprise](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ug3906200f-95c6-4a6b-90b1-f928c85961c6&topicId=con7d418007-d283-41eb-8ea6-6d28e80c161d.html&_LANG=enus) article for more information.
- ChatGPT: The Docusign connector in ChatGPT provides an advanced AI chatbot and virtual assistant. See the [Bringing Our Leading Contract AI to ChatGPT](https://www.docusign.com/blog/docusign-contract-ai-chatgpt) article for more information.

Requirements

- Anthropic Claude: Requires an Anthropic Claude Pro, Team, or Enterprise account.
- Google Gemini: Requires a Google Gemini Enterprise, Premier, or Business plan.
- ChatGPT: Requires ChatGPT Pro, Team, or Enterprise plan.

1. Access  [Docusign App Center](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center).
2. From the left navigation pane, select IA Connectors and
   select the AI connector that you want.

   ![A screenshot of the App Center AI Connectors apps.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-ai-con-pg.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MDAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.b_d3ihlsLoE6LXsJxtKhFCUxszMIx-fzPg0mOp3ok5U&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the App Center AI Connectors apps.")
3. Select an AI connector to display the app's Detail page.

   ![A screenshot of the App Center Claude Cowork detail page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-claude-ai-det-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MDAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.b_d3ihlsLoE6LXsJxtKhFCUxszMIx-fzPg0mOp3ok5U&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the App Center Claude Cowork detail page.")
4. On the app detail page, select Join Waitlist.

   You are redirected to the Docusign MCP Beta Program: Request Access external site to access the MCP Server. See the [Build with Docusign MCP Server (Beta)](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/mcp-server/?utm_source=google&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_NEW_2401_SEMBranded&utm_term=docusign%20mcp&campaignid=23603370651&adgroupid=194748469858&location=9198112&device=c&matchtype=p&extensionid=&creative=798619703005&keyword=docusign%20mcp&placement=&network=g&gclid=CjwKCAjw5ZXQBhBdEiwAI5XVWQTzBj5923Le_p3zGKAXzdbxEgXEIgqEwEd7hGkzMZnIrl0oCZzIfRoC_AwQAvD_BwE&gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=23603370651&gbraid=0AAAAADicDKv84OcGWQ2OMFJBG1oPYGzJh&gclid=CjwKCAjw5ZXQBhBdEiwAI5XVWQTzBj5923Le_p3zGKAXzdbxEgXEIgqEwEd7hGkzMZnIrl0oCZzIfRoC_AwQAvD_BwE) article for information about this program.
5. Select Start  to sign up access for the Beta program to use the AI connector.

title: Configure a Read from HubSpot Step
heading_path: Configure a Read from HubSpot Step
text:
Learn to add and configure a Read from HubSpot step to import data from HubSpot
records.

Before you begin
:   - Required: Installation of the HubSpot app from
      the Docusign App Center. Review the [Installing Extension Applications for
      Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=bgb1697668738892.html) article for more information.
    - Required: [Workflows Create
      permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) or be an account
      administrator.
    - Required: Create a workflow as described in [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus). Add
      steps like [invitation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=kfx1698167344720.html&_LANG=enus),
      [web forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus), [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uww1698167288211.html&_LANG=enus) and
      more to gather recipients, documents, and merge fields for your
      workflow.

You can use a Read from HubSpot step to import HubSpot data to
your workflow. Administrators link workflow fields to HubSpot fields
in the Read from HubSpot step.

When task recipients open envelopes, they see HubSpot data imported from the record.
Importing HubSpot data improves efficiency and reduces errors by eliminating manual
data entry.

Example: You build a workflow with a Read from
HubSpot step. In the Read from HubSpot step, you link to a HubSpot
record's Amount field. When a workflow recipient opens a
contract, they see the record's Amount field pre-filled.

Use this procedure to create a Read from HubSpot step:

1. Add a Read from HubSpot step as a Docusign administrator:
   1. Go to Agreements > Workflows and select Edit next to a workflow
      name.

      The workflow editor page displays.
   2. Select the Add a step
      ![A screenshot displays a plus sign inside of a square.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_add_step_plus_sign.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MDcsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.LC4ncfNBW5LCuNXwM0HxalUzCn1ptc0UawogB6AzXJY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a plus sign inside of a square.") icon.

      The Add New Step dialog box displays.
   3. Search for steps: Enter `Read from
      HubSpot` in the search field and select the
      Read from HubSpot step that displays.

      The workflow editor page displays with the Read from HubSpot
      step added.
2. Name your Read from HubSpot step:
   1. Select Configure on the Read from HubSpot
      step.

      The configuration dialog box displays.
   2. Step Name: Keep or edit the name. It is helpful
      to add the name of the HubSpot object, like `Company - Read
      from HubSpot`.
3. Where will you read from?
   1. Connection: Choose a HubSpot account that you
      connected to Workflow Builder. Multiple options display if you ​connect more than one HubSpot
      account​​​.
   2. (Optional) Add New Connection: Select this
      button if you want to [connect more than one HubSpot
      organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=ogk1719256147008.html&_LANG=enus).
   3. (Optional) Manage Connections: Select this
      button if you want to [refresh your HubSpot connection to Docusign](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=nta1727383289518&topicId=sqw1734725367259.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. HubSpot object: Search for a HubSpot object or
      choose one from the dropdown menu.
   5. Select Next.

title: Configure a Read from HubSpot Step
heading_path: Configure a Read from HubSpot Step
text:
om/s/document-item?bundleId=nta1727383289518&topicId=sqw1734725367259.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. HubSpot object: Search for a HubSpot object or
      choose one from the dropdown menu.
   5. Select Next.

   ![A screenshot displays a Read from HubSpot workflow step with the Connection and HubSpot object fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_HubSpot_Read_Where_will_you_read_from_object.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MDcsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.LC4ncfNBW5LCuNXwM0HxalUzCn1ptc0UawogB6AzXJY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Read from HubSpot workflow step with the Connection and HubSpot object fields highlighted.")
4. Which fields would you like to read?: Select workflow
   fields to map to HubSpot fields. When workflows start, Docusign adds this data to agreements.
   1. Select Add or Remove Fields.

      The Add or Remove Fields page displays.
   2. Select the check box next to each HubSpot field that you'd like to use
      as a workflow variable. You'll be able to use these fields in later
      steps.

      Tip: When you select objects on the
      left, related objects open. The Source column
      displays each field's object path.![A screen capture displays the Add or Remove Fields page with related objects highlighted and fields selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Read_from_HubSpot_Add_or_Remove_Fields.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MDcsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.LC4ncfNBW5LCuNXwM0HxalUzCn1ptc0UawogB6AzXJY&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the Add or Remove Fields page with related objects highlighted and fields selected.")
   3. Select Save and Exit.

      The Add or Remove Fields dialog box closes. The fields that you
      selected display.
   4. Select Next.
5. Which records are you reading from?: Create a rule that
   matches HubSpot fields with equivalent workflow fields. Workflows use
   this rule to read HubSpot data. You can create as many rules as you want:
   1. HubSpot field: Select a HubSpot field to match
      with a Docusign field.

      Note: When you select a
      (reference) type field, a new field opens
      below it. Choose a child object from the new field.
   2. Operator: Choose an option like Equal
      to, Less than, and so on.
   3. Workflow field: Select a Docusign field to match with your HubSpot field:

      - Manual entry: Add text for your new
        variable and select Save.

        Note: The name that you enter must exactly
        match an existing field. For example, the rule fails if you
        enter CompanyName for the
        Company\_Name
        variable.
      - Select a field from the start step or another step in this
        workflow:

        Note: Steps like Read
        from Salesforce and Read from
        HubSpot display fields from up to five
        records. If more than five records are present, HubSpot
        logic decides which five to display. Selecting fields from
        multiple records helps you automate processes like mapping
        multiple HubSpot contacts to multiple Docusign participants.![A screenshot displays read step fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_Salesforce_Read_Workflow_field_selections.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MDcsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.LC4ncfNBW5LCuNXwM0HxalUzCn1ptc0UawogB6AzXJY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays read step fields highlighted.")

title: Configure a Read from HubSpot Step
heading_path: Configure a Read from HubSpot Step
text:
I6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MDcsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.LC4ncfNBW5LCuNXwM0HxalUzCn1ptc0UawogB6AzXJY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays read step fields highlighted.")

        You can use the fields that you select here in
        later steps like Collect Data with
        Webforms or Send Documents for
        Signature.
   4. Select Done to save your rule.

      ![A screenshot displays a rule with Annual Revenue greater than or equal to one million dollars.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_HubSpot_Read_Which_records_are_you_reading_rule.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MDcsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.LC4ncfNBW5LCuNXwM0HxalUzCn1ptc0UawogB6AzXJY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a rule with Annual Revenue greater than or equal to one million dollars.")
6. (Optional) Add more rules:
   - +AND: Workflow Builder only imports HubSpot data that
     matches all of your rules.
   - +OR: Workflow Builder imports HubSpot data that matches
     any of your rules.
7. How many records should this step return?:
   - A single record: Returns the first record that
     matches your rule.
   - Multiple records: Returns up to five records that
     match your rule.

     Note: If more than five records are
     present, HubSpot logic decides which five to display.
8. (Optional) Preview this step: See examples of how this
   rule will work with up to ten HubSpot records:
   1. Select the Preview this step link.

      The Add Data to Preview Step page displays.
   2. Keep or edit the data provided in the field.
   3. Select Preview HubSpot Data.

      ![A screenshot displays the Read from HubSpot Add Data to Preview Step page of a Read from HubSpot workflow step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/Workflow_Builder_for_HubSpot_Read_Step_Add_Data_to_Preview_Step.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE5MDcsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.LC4ncfNBW5LCuNXwM0HxalUzCn1ptc0UawogB6AzXJY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Read from HubSpot Add Data to Preview Step page of a Read from HubSpot workflow step.")

      The page refreshes, displaying HubSpot data that your Read from
      HubSpot rule found.
   4. Select Close.

      The Add Data to Preview Step page closes.
   5. Select Apply.

   The Read From HubSpot column closes. The workflow page displays
   your new step.

You have completed the process. You have built a Read from
HubSpot step in your workflow. When this step is triggered in a workflow, it
imports field data from the HubSpot records that you mapped.

title: Configure a Stores Files in Microsoft OneDrive in an Agreement Workflow
heading_path: Configure a Stores Files in Microsoft OneDrive in an Agreement Workflow
text:
Learn how to configure a Microsoft OneDrive store files step in a Docusign agreement workflow.

Use the Microsoft OneDrive (OneDrive) file stores step to save documents generated by workflow steps. Generally, you position your archive step at the end of your workflow to archive completed documents.

Note: In addition to file archive, you can upload content to Docusign Agreement Manager for changes and revisions.

- See the [Agreement Uploads and Ingestion﻿](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=rrs1712255668350.html&_LANG=enus) article to learn about using
  Agreement Manager.
- See the [Upload Documents from Cloud Storage Locations﻿](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pqz1702943441912&topicId=lxo1726792845151.html&_LANG=enus) article to access and upload files.

Restriction: Do not use a OneDrive file archive step if your workflow uses an eSignature step with multiple participants and your first signer is designated as an embedded signer. In this scenario, the first signer goes directly to sign from a prior workflow step. Documents are archived directly after the embedded signer finishes signing and would not include the actions of any subsequent participants.

1. Create a new workflow as described in the [Create a Basic Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=uhm1697826856678.html&_LANG=enus) article.
2. Select Add a step >  Store files with OneDrive >  Configure.
3. Select the file to archive and select Apply.
4. Specify where to archive the file:
   1. Select the connection.
   2. Select the site.
   3. Select a drive.
   4. (Optional) Select a folder on the drive by choosing Add Folder. All existing folders are listed, as well as an option to create a new folder.
5. Specify a name for your archive. Use the text and variable controls to build your file name.

   ![A screenshot of the OneDrive Archive naming dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/acarchivewhat.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4OTAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.KB4OzraIOjzzi2IIq7iCrTK-u5HuitNlDp100UWNYuU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the OneDrive Archive naming dialog box.")
6. Review the completed file path and select Apply.

Your OneDrive file archive step is configured.

title: Use App Detail Pages to Learn About Docusign Apps
heading_path: Use App Detail Pages to Learn About Docusign Apps
text:
Use a Docusign app's detail page to view important information such as
an app summary, features, permissions, and resources.

Each Docusign app has a detail page with important information about the
app, such as a preview features, permissions, required plans, resources.![A screenshot of a sample Docusign App Detail page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-use-this-app-det-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE3OTEsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.jV9Oa48Ka4qFtTXG1jIj_nF0oQiIJf1AS2uOF-HR9Q0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample Docusign App Detail page.")

title: Use App Detail Pages to Learn About Docusign Apps
heading_path: Use App Detail Pages to Learn About Docusign Apps
text:
The app detail page includes this information:

- Name
- Overview
- Use This App: Select the Docusign feature that you want to use with the app to access the page on which you use the app. For example, selecting Open in Workflow Builder opens a new workflow.
- [Docusign plan requirements](https://ecom.docusign.com/plans-and-pricing/esignature?utm_source=google&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_NEW_2401_SEMBranded&utm_term=docusign%20plans&campaignid=14964582893&adgroupid=127314395926&location=9198112&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=615714019859&keyword=docusign%20plans&placement=&network=g&gclid=Cj0KCQjwjJrCBhCXARIsAI5x66U-Tq5EeW33f-pTTNzCzGNWdogOf4m3z9WCSqCcG5BIia90eYEx7uEaAqTEEALw_wcB&gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=14964582893&gbraid=0AAAAADicDKsNWbtN78PfC2n0xdDWf3d-7)
- App Version number
- [Available Regions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=con81e64917-4912-40c5-a4aa-69f2053f312a.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about finding the global regions where Docusign apps are available. These apps are currently deployed across five different datacenters: U.S., CA, AU, EU, and JP."):
  The global regions where Docusign apps are available.
- Certifications: [FedRAMP-certified
  apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=cone7f31b50-43c1-40a1-b6ed-d7ae1425ce61.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to identify FedRAMP-certified apps in the Docusign App Center.") if available
- Developer (who builds the app)
- Resources
- Requirements: Any app prerequisites
- [Workflow
  templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=con8f35c78f-56b7-466c-91e6-c0ea7d03c516.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to select and initiate a Docusign Workflow Builder template from the Docusign App Center page.") that support this app
- Works with: Docusign features where you use this app
  - Agreement Actions
  - Agreement Manager
  - CLM
  - eSignature
  - Workflow Builder
- Pricing: Option to view [available pricing plans](https://ecom.docusign.com/plans-and-pricing/iam?utm_source=google&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=GBL_XX_PRF_NEW_2401_SEMBranded&utm_term=docusign%20plans&campaignid=14964582893&adgroupid=127314395926&location=9198112&device=c&matchtype=e&extensionid=&creative=615714019859&keyword=docusign%20plans&placement=&network=g&gclid=Cj0KCQjwjJrCBhCXARIsAI5x66U-Tq5EeW33f-pTTNzCzGNWdogOf4m3z9WCSqCcG5BIia90eYEx7uEaAqTEEALw_wcB&gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=14964582893&gbraid=0AAAAADicDKsNWbtN78PfC2n0xdDWf3d-7)

title: Use App Detail Pages to Learn About Docusign Apps
heading_path: Use App Detail Pages to Learn About Docusign Apps
text:
Depending on the app's status, you can:

- Install an app by selecting Install as described in the [Install Apps in the App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install Docusign extension apps in the Docusign App Center.") article.
- Manage an installed app by selecting Manage as described in
  the [Manage Apps in the App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=zxa1698170686717.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view, install, or uninstall Docusign App Center apps.") article.

title: Non-admin Users: Work with Approved Apps
heading_path: Non-admin Users: Work with Approved Apps
text:
As a Docusign account user, learn how to find and manage Docusign apps you’re approved to use.

As a Docusign non-admin user, you can find and manage Docusign apps you are approved to use.

Depending on your organization, these types of apps are available:

- Admin-approved apps are prebuilt Docusign apps approved by your account
  administrator.
- Private Apps are custom apps created exclusively by and
  for your organization.

1. From the [Docusign App Center](https://apps.docusign.com/app-center) home page, select
   the Approved for you filter.
2. Select Admin-approved apps from the menu.

   ![A screenshot of a sample approved for you menu for non-admin users.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-non-approved4u.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NDAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.spVXChUENtivPeMZZvCrSV4ywmqy564gO5_0KSzi2gE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample approved for you menu for non-admin users.")

   The Installed Apps approved for you  page
   displays.

   ![A screenshot of a non-admin user accessing approved apps.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-app-apps-non-dash.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NDAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.spVXChUENtivPeMZZvCrSV4ywmqy564gO5_0KSzi2gE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a non-admin user accessing approved apps.")
3. Select a specific app to display the [App Details Page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=xkg1702568563800.html&_LANG=enus "Use a Docusign app's detail page to view important information such as an app summary, features, permissions, and resources.") page for detailed information about the
   app.

   Depending on the app, you can:

   - Install App: Install the app for
     use with your Docusign features as
     described in the [Install Apps in theDocusign App
     Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install Docusign extension apps in the Docusign App Center.") article.

     ![A screenshot of selecting Install App from the app listing page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-aa-non-admin-detail-install-app.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NDAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.spVXChUENtivPeMZZvCrSV4ywmqy564gO5_0KSzi2gE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of selecting Install App from the app listing page.")
   - Use This App: Select this option
     to go to the Docusign feature
     where you can use the app. For example, selecting
     the Smarty email verification app displays the
     Agreements page. Here you can select Smarty data
     verification fields.

     ![A screenshot of selecting Use This App from the app's detail page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-new-non-adm-appr-detail-use-this.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NDAsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.spVXChUENtivPeMZZvCrSV4ywmqy564gO5_0KSzi2gE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of selecting Use This App from the app's detail page.")
   - Manage Connections to access the
     [Connections
     page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=xyw1716318604389.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create and manage Docusign app connections. Use connections to access different accounts and environments.").

title: Work with Docusign App Connections
text:
Learn how to create and manage Docusign app connections. Use
connections to access different accounts and environments.

With Docusign app connections, you can select different accounts
when creating workflow processes.

You can perform these connection actions:

- Add a new connection
- Manage Connections
  - Edit connection name
  - Delete a connection
  - Manage Field Mappings: Review the suggested mappings to ensure that the data is
    correctly mapped to Docusign IAM fields
  - Reload objects: Refresh the Connections display to view the latest connections
    activity
- Refresh

  Note: This function is not available for all
  apps.
- View connection Details: Select an existing connection to view the app's detail page
  and view connection status
- Uninstall the app: Uninstall the app from the App Center

  Attention: When you uninstall an app, workflows and templates that use the app
  are permanently affected.

title: Work with Docusign App Connections
text:
Your role determines the connection types that you can create and manage.

- Administrator: Private or shared connections
- Account user (no Administrator permission): Private connections

title: Work with Docusign App Connections
text:
You can create Docusign apps connections for use in Docusign agreement workflows for different user credentials.

Here are the available app connection types:

Shared
:   Created by an Admin. Any account user can view and use the connection.

Private
:   Created by an Admin or an account user. Only the account user who created
    the connection can view and use the connection.

For the Salesforce app, you can also create connections based on the environment
type.

- Demos (Sandbox) account
- Production account

title: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 7: Create Cross-Scope Privilege Records
heading_path: Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance, Step 7: Create Cross-Scope Privilege Records
text:
Learn how to create cross-scope privilege records so that Docusign can
access ServiceNow tables.

Exchange data between Docusign and ServiceNow with Docusign IAM for ServiceNow. Use the integration to kick off software and hardware
procurement requests and other downstream workflows.

This topic shows how to create cross-scope privilege records in ServiceNow.

Important: To edit a table, you must be in the same application
scope. A cross-application scope allows users to access tables in other scopes while
remaining in their current one.

1. Select the All tab from the top menu. Search for and
   select Application Cross-Scope Access.

   The Cross scope privileges page
   displays.
2. Select New at the top right to create and configure a
   new privilege.

   The Cross scope privilege New record page
   displays.
3. Set the Target Scope to the scope of the target table.
   This is the table that you are granting access to.

   If the company table is under the **Global**scope, then the target scope
   mut also be Global.
4. For the Target Name, enter the name of the target table in
   the Namebox.
5. Set the Target Type to
   Table.
6. Set the Application to Docusign
   IAM.
7. Select the Operation (read,
   create, write).
8. Set the Status to Allowed.
9. Select Submit.
10. Repeat these steps to create a cross-scope privilege record for all three operations.

You completed this task.

title: Configure a ServiceNow Writeback Step in an Agreement Workflow
heading_path: Configure a ServiceNow Writeback Step in an Agreement Workflow
text:
Learn about using the ServiceNow app to run multiple business applications per instance
through Docusign agreement workflows.

ServiceNow app instances run multiple business applications per instance, with each application
sharing data. You can use ServiceNow through Docusign Workflow Builder
workflows.

You must be an account administrator or have Workflows: Create user permissions as
described in the [Workflow Builder Roles and Permission
Requirements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gbb1696973048215.html&_LANG=enus) article.

Configuring ServiceNow for Docusign involves:

- [Set Up the Docusign App Center ServiceNow Instance](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=qau1712843715884.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set up the ServiceNow instance for use in the Docusign App Center.")
- [Troubleshoot Installing the Docusign IAM for ServiceNow App](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=bur1727290839878.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to troubleshoot issues with installing Docusign IAM for ServiceNow.")
- [Create the ServiceNow Writeback
  Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ejl1711393084084.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create the ServiceNow writeback step to use a ServiceNow instance in Docusign Workflow Builder workflows.")

title: Create Docusign App Connections
text:
Learn how to create Docusign apps connections for different user
credentials in Docusign agreement workflows. You can create connections
that are shared by all account users or
used
privately by the user who created
the
app connection.

You can create Docusign
app
connections for use in Docusign agreement workflows. See the [Manage Docusign App
Connections](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=xyw1716318604389.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create and manage Docusign app connections. Use connections to access different accounts and environments.") article for information about user permissions and connection
types.

Connection types

Depending on your user credentials, you can create
these
app connection types:

- Shared: Created by an Admin. Any account user can view
  and use the connection.
- Private: Created by an account user or an Admin. Only the
  account user who created the connection can view and use the connection.

Before you begin

Install and configure the app as described in the [Install Apps in the Docusign App Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=ctg1698170340729.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install Docusign extension apps in the Docusign App Center.") article.

title: Create Docusign App Connections
text:
As a user with Administrator privileges, you can create shared
app
connections. Any account user can view and use in Docusign platform services, such as Workflow Builder and Agreement Manager.

1. From the Docusign App Center Manage page, select an app
   to display the app's detail page as shown in this example.

   ![a screenshot of a detail page with the Manage menu to access app connections.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-new-det-w-man-con-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4NTQsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.bGJc63oVa4YaKWIgtlH1h3euRAzT5nw7p66PMKW1icg&_LANG=enus "a screenshot of a detail page with the Manage menu to access app connections.")
2. Select Manage Connections to display the app's Connections page.

   Note: If you have more than one account, select the
   account for which you want to add a connection.
3. Select New Connection.
4. Salesforce only select a Salesforce account environment.

   Choose from:
   - Salesforce Production
   - Salesforce Sandbox
5. At the Select Account Type page, Select the Shared connection type and select
   Next.
6. At the Name Connection page, select a name for your connection.

   Tip: Change the default name to something you can easily identify.
7. If prompted, select Login to
   connect
   your app.

   You have created a shared app connection to use with platform services.

   See the [Configure Docusign Apps in an Agreement Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=hno1706034267152.html&_LANG=enus "You can integrate Docusign apps to automate your agreement workflows and boost productivity.")
   article for more information.

title: Create Docusign App Connections
text:
As a user without Administrator privileges, you can only create
private
app connections. Only you can view and use
these
app connections in agreement platform processes.

1. From the Docusign App Center Manage page, select an app
   to display the app's Detail Listing page.
2. Select Log in and enter your credentials.
3. If prompted, select Connect Account to enable access
   to the app account.

   Note: If you have more than one account, select the
   account where you want to add a connection.
4. At the Select Account Type page, select the Private
   connection type and select Next.
5. At the Name Connection page, select a name for your connection.

   Tip: Change the default name to something
   that you can easily identify.
6. If prompted, select Login to connect to your
   app.

   - Select Allow
     to confirm access to the account.
   - Enter configuration or authorization information specific to your
     app and select Next.

   You have created a private app connection to use with platform services.

   See the [Configure Docusign Apps in an Agreement Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ous1698169987748&topicId=hno1706034267152.html&_LANG=enus "You can integrate Docusign apps to automate your agreement workflows and boost productivity.")
   article for more information.

title: Manage Apps in the Docusign App Center
heading_path: Manage Apps in the Docusign App Center
text:
Learn how to view, install, or uninstall Docusign App Center apps.

In Docusign App Center, you can view,
install, and uninstall apps from the Managed Apps page.

![A screenshot of a sample Docusign Manage Apps Page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-conn-dash-inst-apps.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MTMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.Z3s6DZlJObHsKMDzwONecKm5_MVpWkcf22jdFEGVqdA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample Docusign Manage Apps Page.")

The Manage Apps page provides a left-pane menu that provides these
actions:

- Installed Apps: Select this option to display installed
  apps for accounts under your organization.

  The display includes details, such
  as activity status and version number.
- Approved Apps: Select this option to display apps that
  are approved for all users in your account.

  As an administrator, use the
  access toggle switch to permit non-administrator users in the account to
  install the apps.
- View all Admin settings: Select this option to access
  your account's Admin dashboard, as described in the [What is Docusign
  Admin?](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=jte1583359129941.html&_LANG=enus)
  article.

  Use the Docusign Admin page to perform tasks such
  as setting user permissions for your apps.

To perform management activities from the Manage Apps page:

1. Access  [Docusign App Center](https://apps-d.docusign.com/app-center).
2. Select Log in.
3. Select Manage Apps to display your apps.
4. Select an app to display its app detail page.

   You can:

   - View a detailed app description.
   - Display app details, as shown in this example.

     ![A screenshot showing sample App Details page information.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/ac-newest-details-works-with.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MTMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.Z3s6DZlJObHsKMDzwONecKm5_MVpWkcf22jdFEGVqdA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing sample App Details page information.")
5. Optionally, you can uninstall an app by selecting the app and selecting Manage App  > Uninstall App.

   You are prompted to confirm the action.

   ![A screenshot of the Docusign App Center uninstall app dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ous1698169987748/page/images/prompt-to-uninstall-an-app.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE4MTMsInNoZWFmIjoib3VzMTY5ODE2OTk4Nzc0OCJ9.Z3s6DZlJObHsKMDzwONecKm5_MVpWkcf22jdFEGVqdA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign App Center uninstall app dialog box.")

   Important: If you uninstall an app, or remove the app's authorization, the operation fails. For example, apps you use in a Docusign Workflow Builder workflow step, or Data Verification fields.
6. Enter UNINSTALL and select
   Uninstall to remove the app from Docusign App Center.

   Note: Use all uppercase letters to activate the
   Uninstall button.
